- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-faith-006 (#1180) — candidate entailment-judge prompts that teach specific->general WIDENING.
2	
3	F02 (BB5-F02, clinical overgeneralization): the real entailment judge ENTAILS a drb_76 claim that
4	WIDENS a specific strain/population to a broad class ("S. boulardii not recommended in
5	immunocompromised" -> "current evidence advises against routine probiotic use"). Enforcement cannot
6	fix an LLM-behavior gap; per operator policy the fix is a WIDENING-AWARE judge prompt, EMPIRICALLY
7	BAKED OFF against a labeled set (`tests/fixtures/widening_labeled_set.json`) and picked by
8	precision/recall — never a hand-chosen prompt.
9	
10	This module holds ONLY the candidate prompt VARIANTS (pure strings) + the pure scoring helpers. The
11	BASELINE prompt stays in `entailment_judge._ENTAILMENT_PROMPT` (this module never imports it, to
12	avoid a cycle); `entailment_judge._select_entailment_prompt()` returns the baseline by default
13	(``PG_ENTAILMENT_PROMPT_VARIANT`` unset/"baseline" -> byte-identical) and a candidate only when
14	explicitly selected. The bakeoff (`scripts/dr_benchmark/widening_prompt_bakeoff.py`) scores every
15	variant against the labeled set with the REAL judge (spend-gated) and the winning variant is wired by
16	setting the env in the run slate.
17	
18	Each variant MUST keep the same ``{span}`` / ``{sentence}`` format fields and the STRICT-JSON output
19	contract so it is a drop-in replacement.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	# The shared widening NEUTRAL exemplars, phrased differently per candidate. The directional rule is
25	# the same: when the SENTENCE generalizes a SPECIFIC entity / strain / drug member / subgroup /
26	# population / mechanism / setting in the SPAN to a BROADER class, that is specific->general = NEUTRAL
27	# (NOT entailed). The opposite direction (span general, sentence a faithful subset) stays ENTAILED.
28	
29	# Candidate A — terse rule addition + one strain exemplar.
30	_WIDEN_A = """You are a strict entailment judge. You will be given a SPAN of source text and a SENTENCE that cites that span. Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE.
31	
32	Rules:
33	- ENTAILED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase is allowed, and a faithful SUBSET of the span (general span -> narrower true sentence, dropping a CI bound) is still ENTAILED.
34	- NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE introduces a fact, entity, mechanism, or specificity NOT present in the SPAN (e.g. SPAN says "GLP-1 RAs", SENTENCE says "semaglutide"; SPAN says "adipocyte metabolism", SENTENCE adds "lipid metabolism"). ALSO NEUTRAL when the SENTENCE GENERALIZES a SPECIFIC entity/strain/drug/subgroup/population/setting in the SPAN to a BROADER class (specific->general is NOT entailed: SPAN "S. boulardii not recommended in immunocompromised", SENTENCE "probiotics are not recommended" is NEUTRAL).
35	- CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.
36	
37	Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
38	{{"verdict": "ENTAILED" | "NEUTRAL" | "CONTRADICTED", "reason": "<one short sentence>"}}
39	
40	SPAN:
41	{span}
42	
43	SENTENCE:
44	{sentence}
45	
46	JSON:"""
47	
48	# Candidate B — explicit "direction test" framing + two exemplars (strain + subgroup).
49	_WIDEN_B = """You are a strict entailment judge. You will be given a SPAN of source text and a SENTENCE that cites that span. Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE.
50	
51	First apply the SCOPE-DIRECTION test: if the SENTENCE is BROADER than the SPAN — generalizing a specific entity, drug, strain, subgroup, population, mechanism, or study setting to a wider class — the SPAN does NOT entail it. Only a sentence whose scope is EQUAL TO or NARROWER THAN the span (and otherwise faithful) can be ENTAILED.
52	
53	Rules:
54	- ENTAILED: every assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN and the SENTENCE is no broader than the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase and faithful subsets are ENTAILED.
55	- NEUTRAL: the SENTENCE adds a fact/entity/mechanism/specificity absent from the SPAN, OR widens the span's specific scope to a broader class. Examples: SPAN "GLP-1 RAs", SENTENCE "semaglutide" (NEUTRAL); SPAN "S. boulardii not recommended in immunocompromised patients", SENTENCE "probiotics are not recommended" (NEUTRAL, strain+population widened); SPAN "in adults 65+, hospitalization fell 40%", SENTENCE "in adults, hospitalization fell 40%" (NEUTRAL, subgroup widened).
56	- CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.
57	
58	Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
59	{{"verdict": "ENTAILED" | "NEUTRAL" | "CONTRADICTED", "reason": "<one short sentence>"}}
60	
61	SPAN:
62	{span}
63	
64	SENTENCE:
65	{sentence}
66	
67	JSON:"""
68	
69	# Candidate C — checklist framing to reduce false-drops on legitimate paraphrase.
70	_WIDEN_C = """You are a strict entailment judge. You will be given a SPAN of source text and a SENTENCE that cites that span. Decide whether the SPAN entails the SENTENCE.
71	
72	Decide in two steps:
73	1. SCOPE: Is the SENTENCE's claimed entity/drug/strain/subgroup/population/mechanism/setting the SAME AS or NARROWER THAN the SPAN's? If the SENTENCE is BROADER (generalizes the span's specific item to a wider class), the answer is NEUTRAL regardless of how similar the wording is.
74	2. SUPPORT: If scope is OK, is every factual assertion in the SENTENCE supported by the SPAN? If yes -> ENTAILED; if it adds an unsupported fact/specificity -> NEUTRAL; if it conflicts -> CONTRADICTED.
75	
76	Conservative paraphrase, voice changes, and faithful SUBSETS of the span (e.g. dropping a CI bound, same scope) are ENTAILED. Do NOT mark a same-scope faithful paraphrase NEUTRAL.
77	
78	Examples of widening (NEUTRAL): SPAN "S. boulardii not recommended in immunocompromised" -> SENTENCE "probiotics are not recommended"; SPAN "GLP-1 RAs reduced HbA1c" -> SENTENCE "semaglutide reduced HbA1c"; SPAN "approved for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis" -> SENTENCE "effective for psoriasis".
79	
80	Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
81	{{"verdict": "ENTAILED" | "NEUTRAL" | "CONTRADICTED", "reason": "<one short sentence>"}}
82	
83	SPAN:
84	{span}
85	
86	SENTENCE:
87	{sentence}
88	
89	JSON:"""
90	
91	
92	# Variant registry. "baseline" is intentionally ABSENT here — the selector returns the canonical
93	# `entailment_judge._ENTAILMENT_PROMPT` for baseline so it is byte-identical and there is one source
94	# of truth for the current prompt.
95	WIDENING_VARIANTS: dict[str, str] = {
96	    "widen_a": _WIDEN_A,
97	    "widen_b": _WIDEN_B,
98	    "widen_c": _WIDEN_C,
99	}
100	
101	_VALID_VERDICTS = ("ENTAILED", "NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED")
102	
103	
104	def score_predictions(rows: list[dict], predictions: list[str]) -> dict:
105	    """Pure scorer for a candidate's predictions over the labeled set.
106	
107	    ``rows`` are the labeled triples (each with a ``gold`` verdict + ``category``); ``predictions``
108	    are the candidate's verdicts in the same order. Returns the confusion matrix plus the two binding
109	    metrics: ``widening_neutral_recall`` (recall on gold=NEUTRAL widening rows — the fix target) and
110	    ``entailed_precision`` (fraction of gold=ENTAILED rows kept ENTAILED — the no-false-drop guard).
111	    No LLM, no I/O.
112	    """
113	    if len(rows) != len(predictions):
114	        raise ValueError("rows and predictions length mismatch")
115	    confusion: dict[tuple[str, str], int] = {}
116	    gold_neutral = pred_neutral_on_gold_neutral = 0
117	    gold_entailed = pred_entailed_on_gold_entailed = 0
118	    # Codex slice-4 P1: a CONTRADICTED row predicted ENTAILED is the §-1.1-LETHAL failure (the
119	    # prompt accepts a direct contradiction). Count it so the winner picker can reject any variant
120	    # that ever does this, regardless of its widening recall / entailed precision.
121	    contradiction_accepted = 0
122	    for row, pred in zip(rows, predictions):
123	        gold = str(row.get("gold", "")).upper()
124	        pred = str(pred).upper()
125	        confusion[(gold, pred)] = confusion.get((gold, pred), 0) + 1
126	        if gold == "NEUTRAL":
127	            gold_neutral += 1
128	            if pred == "NEUTRAL":
129	                pred_neutral_on_gold_neutral += 1
130	        elif gold == "ENTAILED":
131	            gold_entailed += 1
132	            if pred == "ENTAILED":
133	                pred_entailed_on_gold_entailed += 1
134	        elif gold == "CONTRADICTED" and pred == "ENTAILED":
135	            contradiction_accepted += 1
136	    widening_neutral_recall = (
137	        pred_neutral_on_gold_neutral / gold_neutral if gold_neutral else 0.0
138	    )
139	    entailed_precision = (
140	        pred_entailed_on_gold_entailed / gold_entailed if gold_entailed else 0.0
141	    )
142	    return {
143	        "confusion": {f"{g}->{p}": n for (g, p), n in sorted(confusion.items())},
144	        "gold_neutral": gold_neutral,
145	        "gold_entailed": gold_entailed,
146	        "widening_neutral_recall": round(widening_neutral_recall, 4),
147	        "entailed_precision": round(entailed_precision, 4),
148	        "contradiction_accepted": contradiction_accepted,
149	    }
150	
151	
152	def pick_winner(scores_by_variant: dict[str, dict], *, min_entailed_precision: float = 0.95) -> str:
153	    """Select the variant with the highest ``widening_neutral_recall`` subject to
154	    ``entailed_precision >= min_entailed_precision`` (no faithfulness regression on legitimate
155	    support) AND zero ``contradiction_accepted`` (the §-1.1-LETHAL gate: a prompt that ever grades a
156	    gold CONTRADICTED row as ENTAILED is INELIGIBLE no matter how good its widening recall — Codex
157	    slice-4 P1). Ties break toward higher entailed_precision then variant name. Returns the variant
158	    key, or "baseline" if NO candidate clears both floors (fail-safe: do not regress)."""
159	    eligible = [
160	        (name, s)
161	        for name, s in scores_by_variant.items()
162	        if s.get("entailed_precision", 0.0) >= min_entailed_precision
163	        and int(s.get("contradiction_accepted", 0)) == 0
164	    ]
165	    if not eligible:
166	        return "baseline"
167	    eligible.sort(
168	        key=lambda kv: (
169	            kv[1].get("widening_neutral_recall", 0.0),
170	            kv[1].get("entailed_precision", 0.0),
171	            kv[0],
172	        ),
173	        reverse=True,
174	    )
175	    return eligible[0][0]
176

1	"""
2	Persistent campaign storage backed by SQLite.
3	
4	Stores research campaigns (multi-query batches) so they survive server
5	restarts.  Each campaign holds its queries, per-query status, aggregated
6	results, and optional metadata -- all serialised as JSON TEXT columns.
7	
8	DB path controlled by PG_CAMPAIGN_DB_PATH (default: state/pg_campaigns.sqlite).
9	"""
10	
11	import json
12	import logging
13	import os
14	import time
15	from pathlib import Path
16	from typing import Optional
17	
18	import aiosqlite
19	from dotenv import load_dotenv
20	
21	load_dotenv()
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	# LAW VI -- all paths/thresholds from environment, never hard-coded
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	_DB_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_CACHE_DIR", "state"))
29	_DB_PATH = Path(os.getenv("PG_CAMPAIGN_DB_PATH", str(_DB_DIR / "pg_campaigns.sqlite")))
30	
31	# JSON serialisation helpers
32	_JSON_FIELDS = ("queries_json", "results_json", "metadata_json")
33	
34	
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	# Internal helpers
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	async def _ensure_table(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:
40	    """Create the campaigns table and indices if they do not exist."""
41	    await db.execute("""
42	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS campaigns (
43	            campaign_id   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
44	            name          TEXT NOT NULL,
45	            description   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
46	            queries_json  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
47	            status        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'created',
48	            results_json  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
49	            created_at    REAL NOT NULL,
50	            updated_at    REAL NOT NULL,
51	            metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'
52	        )
53	    """)
54	    await db.execute("""
55	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaigns_status
56	            ON campaigns (status)
57	    """)
58	    await db.execute("""
59	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaigns_created
60	            ON campaigns (created_at DESC)
61	    """)
62	    await db.commit()
63	
64	
65	def _serialize_json_fields(campaign: dict) -> dict:
66	    """Convert Python objects in JSON columns to JSON strings for storage.
67	
68	    Operates on a *copy* so the caller's dict is not mutated.
69	    """
70	    row = dict(campaign)
71	    for field in _JSON_FIELDS:
72	        value = row.get(field)
73	        if value is not None and not isinstance(value, str):
74	            row[field] = json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)
75	    return row
76	
77	
78	def _deserialize_json_fields(row: dict) -> dict:
79	    """Parse JSON TEXT columns back into Python objects."""
80	    result = dict(row)
81	    for field in _JSON_FIELDS:
82	        raw = result.get(field)
83	        if raw is not None and isinstance(raw, str):
84	            try:
85	                result[field] = json.loads(raw)
86	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError) as exc:
87	                logger.warning(
88	                    "[campaign_store] Failed to parse %s for campaign %s: %s",
89	                    field,
90	                    result.get("campaign_id", "?"),
91	                    str(exc)[:200],
92	                )
93	                # Keep the raw string rather than silently dropping data
94	    return result
95	
96	
97	def _row_to_dict(row: aiosqlite.Row) -> dict:
98	    """Convert an aiosqlite.Row to a plain dict, then deserialise JSON fields."""
99	    raw = {
100	        "campaign_id": row["campaign_id"],
101	        "name": row["name"],
102	        "description": row["description"],
103	        "queries_json": row["queries_json"],
104	        "status": row["status"],
105	        "results_json": row["results_json"],
106	        "created_at": row["created_at"],
107	        "updated_at": row["updated_at"],
108	        "metadata_json": row["metadata_json"],
109	    }
110	    return _deserialize_json_fields(raw)
111	
112	
113	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	# Public API
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	async def init_campaign_store() -> None:
118	    """Initialise the campaign store -- create the SQLite table if needed.
119	
120	    Safe to call multiple times (idempotent).  Must be awaited at server
121	    startup before any other campaign_store function is used.
122	    """
123	    _DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
124	    try:
125	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(_DB_PATH)) as db:
126	            await _ensure_table(db)
127	        logger.info(
128	            "[campaign_store] Initialised at %s",
129	            _DB_PATH,
130	        )
131	    except Exception as exc:
132	        logger.error(
133	            "[campaign_store] Failed to initialise database at %s: %s",
134	            _DB_PATH,
135	            str(exc)[:300],
136	        )
137	        raise
138	
139	
140	async def save_campaign(campaign: dict) -> None:
141	    """Insert or update (upsert) a campaign.
142	
143	    ``campaign`` must contain at least ``campaign_id``.  All JSON-column
144	    values (queries_json, results_json, metadata_json) may be passed as
145	    Python objects -- they are serialised automatically.
146	
147	    Raises on database errors (LAW II -- no silent failures).
148	    """
149	    if not campaign.get("campaign_id"):
150	        raise ValueError("campaign dict must contain a non-empty 'campaign_id'")
151	
152	    _DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
153	    row = _serialize_json_fields(campaign)
154	    now = time.time()
155	
156	    # Fill defaults for required columns if the caller omitted them
157	    row.setdefault("name", "")
158	    row.setdefault("description", "")
159	    row.setdefault("queries_json", "[]")
160	    row.setdefault("status", "created")
161	    row.setdefault("results_json", "{}")
162	    row.setdefault("created_at", now)
163	    row.setdefault("updated_at", now)
164	    row.setdefault("metadata_json", "{}")
165	
166	    # Always bump updated_at on save
167	    row["updated_at"] = now
168	
169	    try:
170	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(_DB_PATH)) as db:
171	            await _ensure_table(db)
172	            await db.execute(
173	                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO campaigns
174	                   (campaign_id, name, description, queries_json, status,
175	                    results_json, created_at, updated_at, metadata_json)
176	                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
177	                (
178	                    row["campaign_id"],

308	    fail loudly, never silently no-op on missing data).

1	"""
2	Persistent URL/content cache backed by SQLite.
3	
4	Stores fetched page content to avoid re-fetching URLs across runs.
5	TTL-based expiry (default 7 days).
6	"""
7	
8	import hashlib
9	import logging
10	import os
11	import time
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Optional
14	
15	import aiosqlite
16	from dotenv import load_dotenv
17	
18	load_dotenv()
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	CACHE_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_CACHE_DIR", "state"))
23	CACHE_DB = CACHE_DIR / "pg_content_cache.sqlite"
24	DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS = int(os.getenv("PG_CONTENT_CACHE_TTL_HOURS", "168"))  # 7 days
25	
26	# A1.1: Cap raw HTML storage to prevent SQLite bloat (LAW VI).
27	_RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS", "500000"))  # 500KB
28	
29	
30	def extract_readability_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
31	    """A1.1: Extract article-body HTML from raw page HTML using readability-lxml.
32	
33	    Returns the cleaned article HTML, or empty string if readability-lxml is
34	    not installed or extraction fails. This is a pure function with no side
35	    effects -- safe to call from any context.
36	
37	    Args:
38	        raw_html: The raw HTTP response body (HTML string).
39	
40	    Returns:
41	        Cleaned article-only HTML from readability, or "" on failure.
42	    """
43	    if not raw_html or len(raw_html) < 100:
44	        return ""
45	
46	    try:
47	        from readability import Document as ReadabilityDocument
48	
49	        doc = ReadabilityDocument(raw_html)
50	        readable = doc.summary() or ""
51	        return readable
52	    except ImportError:
53	        # readability-lxml not installed -- graceful degradation
54	        logger.debug(
55	            "[content_cache] A1.1: readability-lxml not installed, "
56	            "skipping readability extraction"
57	        )
58	        return ""
59	    except Exception as exc:
60	        logger.debug(
61	            "[content_cache] A1.1: readability extraction failed: %s",
62	            str(exc)[:200],
63	        )
64	        return ""
65	
66	
67	async def _ensure_table(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:
68	    """Create the cache table if it doesn't exist."""
69	    await db.execute("""
70	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS url_cache (
71	            url TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
72	            content TEXT NOT NULL,
73	            title TEXT DEFAULT '',
74	            content_length INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
75	            fetch_method TEXT DEFAULT 'direct',
76	            fetched_at REAL NOT NULL,
77	            ttl_hours INTEGER DEFAULT 168,
78	            raw_html TEXT DEFAULT '',
79	            readability_html TEXT DEFAULT ''
80	        )
81	    """)
82	    await db.commit()
83	    # Migration: add HTML columns to existing databases (A1.1)
84	    try:
85	        await db.execute(
86	            "ALTER TABLE url_cache ADD COLUMN raw_html TEXT DEFAULT ''"
87	        )
88	        await db.commit()
89	    except Exception:
90	        pass  # Column already exists
91	    try:
92	        await db.execute(
93	            "ALTER TABLE url_cache ADD COLUMN readability_html TEXT DEFAULT ''"
94	        )
95	        await db.commit()
96	    except Exception:
97	        pass  # Column already exists
98	
99	
100	async def get_cached_content(url: str) -> Optional[dict]:
101	    """Retrieve cached content for a URL.
102	
103	    Returns dict with keys: url, content, title, content_length, fetch_method, fetched_at
104	    Returns None if not cached or expired.
105	    """
106	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
107	    try:
108	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
109	            await _ensure_table(db)
110	            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row
111	            cursor = await db.execute(
112	                "SELECT * FROM url_cache WHERE url = ?", (url,)
113	            )
114	            row = await cursor.fetchone()
115	            if row is None:
116	                return None
117	
118	            # Check TTL
119	            fetched_at = row["fetched_at"]
120	            ttl_hours = row["ttl_hours"] or DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS
121	            age_hours = (time.time() - fetched_at) / 3600
122	            if age_hours > ttl_hours:
123	                logger.debug(
124	                    "[content_cache] Expired entry for %s (%.1fh > %dh)",
125	                    url[:60], age_hours, ttl_hours,
126	                )
127	                return None
128	
129	            return {
130	                "url": row["url"],
131	                "content": row["content"],
132	                "title": row["title"],
133	                "content_length": row["content_length"],
134	                "fetch_method": row["fetch_method"],
135	                "fetched_at": row["fetched_at"],
136	                "raw_html": row["raw_html"] if "raw_html" in row.keys() else "",
137	                "readability_html": row["readability_html"] if "readability_html" in row.keys() else "",
138	            }
139	    except Exception as exc:
140	        logger.warning(
141	            "[content_cache] Read failed for %s: %s",
142	            url[:60], str(exc)[:200],
143	        )
144	        return None
145	
146	
147	async def cache_content(
148	    url: str,
149	    content: str,
150	    title: str = "",
151	    fetch_method: str = "direct",
152	    ttl_hours: int = DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS,
153	    raw_html: str = "",
154	    readability_html: str = "",
155	) -> bool:
156	    """Store fetched content in cache.
157	
158	    Args:
159	        url: The source URL.
160	        content: Plaintext extracted content.
161	        title: Page title.
162	        fetch_method: How content was fetched (direct, jina, firecrawl, etc.).
163	        ttl_hours: Time-to-live in hours.
164	        raw_html: Original fetched HTML (as-is from HTTP response).
165	        readability_html: Cleaned HTML via readability algorithm (article body only).
166	
167	    Returns True on success.
168	    """
169	    if not url or not content:
170	        return False
171	
172	    # A1.1: Cap raw HTML to prevent SQLite bloat
173	    if raw_html and len(raw_html) > _RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS:
174	        raw_html = raw_html[:_RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS]
175	    if readability_html and len(readability_html) > _RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS:
176	        readability_html = readability_html[:_RAW_HTML_MAX_CHARS]
177	
178	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
179	    try:
180	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
181	            await _ensure_table(db)
182	            await db.execute(
183	                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO url_cache
184	                   (url, content, title, content_length, fetch_method, fetched_at,
185	                    ttl_hours, raw_html, readability_html)
186	                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
187	                (url, content, title, len(content), fetch_method, time.time(),
188	                 ttl_hours, raw_html, readability_html),
189	            )
190	            await db.commit()
191	        return True
192	    except Exception as exc:
193	        logger.warning(
194	            "[content_cache] Write failed for %s: %s",
195	            url[:60], str(exc)[:200],
196	        )
197	        return False
198	
199	
200	async def get_cache_stats() -> dict:
201	    """Get cache statistics."""
202	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
203	    try:
204	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
205	            await _ensure_table(db)
206	            cursor = await db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM url_cache")
207	            total = (await cursor.fetchone())[0]
208	
209	            cursor = await db.execute(
210	                "SELECT SUM(content_length) FROM url_cache"
211	            )
212	            total_bytes = (await cursor.fetchone())[0] or 0
213	
214	            now = time.time()
215	            cursor = await db.execute(
216	                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM url_cache WHERE (? - fetched_at) / 3600.0 > ttl_hours",
217	                (now,),
218	            )
219	            expired = (await cursor.fetchone())[0]
220	
221	            return {
222	                "total_entries": total,
223	                "total_bytes": total_bytes,
224	                "expired_entries": expired,
225	                "active_entries": total - expired,
226	            }
227	    except Exception as exc:
228	        logger.warning("[content_cache] Stats failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
229	        return {"total_entries": 0, "total_bytes": 0, "expired_entries": 0, "active_entries": 0}
230	
231	
232	async def purge_expired() -> int:
233	    """Remove expired entries. Returns count of purged entries."""
234	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
235	    try:
236	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
237	            await _ensure_table(db)
238	            now = time.time()
239	            cursor = await db.execute(
240	                "DELETE FROM url_cache WHERE (? - fetched_at) / 3600.0 > ttl_hours",
241	                (now,),
242	            )
243	            await db.commit()
244	            purged = cursor.rowcount
245	            if purged > 0:
246	                logger.info("[content_cache] Purged %d expired entries", purged)
247	            return purged
248	    except Exception as exc:
249	        logger.warning("[content_cache] Purge failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
250	        return 0

1	"""
2	Cross-vector knowledge sharing via ChromaDB.
3	
4	Bridges the polaris_graph evidence system with the existing ChromaDB
5	long-term memory (src/memory/chroma_client.py).  High-quality evidence
6	is promoted to LTM-Global for reuse across vectors; existing knowledge
7	can be queried before starting a new research run.
8	
9	Collection: polaris_ltm_global
10	
11	This module is best-effort: if ChromaDB is unavailable the functions
12	degrade gracefully (log a warning, return empty/zero).
13	"""
14	
15	import logging
16	import os
17	import re
18	from pathlib import Path
19	from typing import Optional
20	from urllib.parse import urlparse
21	
22	from dotenv import load_dotenv
23	
24	load_dotenv()
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	LTM_COLLECTION_NAME = "polaris_ltm_global"
29	
30	# Quality tier ordering for comparison
31	_TIER_RANK = {"GOLD": 3, "SILVER": 2, "BRONZE": 1}
32	
33	
34	def _get_chroma_manager():
35	    """Try to obtain the existing ChromaManager singleton.
36	
37	    Returns the manager instance, or None if ChromaDB is not available.
38	    """
39	    try:
40	        from src.memory.chroma_client import get_chroma_manager
41	        manager = get_chroma_manager()
42	        return manager
43	    except Exception as exc:
44	        logger.debug(
45	            "[cross_vector] ChromaManager import failed, "
46	            "trying local ChromaDB client: %s",
47	            str(exc)[:200],
48	        )
49	
50	    # Fallback: create a local PersistentClient
51	    try:
52	        import chromadb
53	        from chromadb.config import Settings
54	
55	        persist_dir = Path(os.getenv("CHROMA_PERSIST_DIR", "data/chroma_db"))
56	        persist_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
57	        client = chromadb.PersistentClient(
58	            path=str(persist_dir),
59	            settings=Settings(
60	                anonymized_telemetry=False,
61	                allow_reset=False,
62	            ),
63	        )
64	        return client
65	    except Exception as exc:
66	        logger.warning(
67	            "[cross_vector] ChromaDB not available: %s", str(exc)[:200],
68	        )
69	        return None
70	
71	
72	def _get_collection(manager):
73	    """Get or create the LTM-Global collection from a manager or raw client.
74	
75	    Handles both ChromaManager (has .get_collection) and raw chromadb.ClientAPI.
76	    Returns the collection object, or None on failure.
77	    """
78	    try:
79	        # ChromaManager path
80	        if hasattr(manager, "get_collection"):
81	            return manager.get_collection(LTM_COLLECTION_NAME, use_embedding=True)
82	        # Raw chromadb client path
83	        if hasattr(manager, "get_or_create_collection"):
84	            return manager.get_or_create_collection(name=LTM_COLLECTION_NAME)
85	        logger.warning("[cross_vector] Unknown manager type: %s", type(manager))
86	        return None
87	    except Exception as exc:
88	        logger.warning(
89	            "[cross_vector] Failed to get collection '%s': %s",
90	            LTM_COLLECTION_NAME, str(exc)[:200],
91	        )
92	        return None
93	
94	
95	def _extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
96	    """Extract the domain from a URL for stats aggregation."""
97	    try:
98	        parsed = urlparse(url)
99	        return parsed.netloc or url[:60]
100	    except Exception:
101	        return url[:60] if url else ""
102	
103	
104	def promote_to_ltm(
105	    evidence_pieces: list[dict],
106	    vector_id: str,
107	    min_quality: str | None = None,
108	    min_faithfulness: float | None = None,
109	) -> int:
110	    """Promote high-quality evidence to ChromaDB LTM-Global.
111	
112	    Filters evidence_pieces by quality tier and faithfulness, then
113	    upserts qualifying items into the polaris_ltm_global collection.
114	
115	    Args:
116	        evidence_pieces: List of evidence dicts. Expected keys:
117	            evidence_id (str), statement (str), source (str/url),
118	            quality_tier (str), faithfulness (float), perspective (str),
119	            relevance_score (float).
120	        vector_id: Source vector identifier for provenance.
121	        min_quality: Minimum quality tier ('GOLD', 'SILVER', 'BRONZE').
122	        min_faithfulness: Minimum faithfulness score (0.0-1.0).
123	
124	    Returns:
125	        Number of evidence pieces promoted. 0 if ChromaDB unavailable.
126	    """
127	    if not evidence_pieces:
128	        return 0
129	
130	    # LAW VI: env var overrides for LTM promotion thresholds
131	    if min_quality is None:
132	        min_quality = os.getenv("PG_LTM_MIN_QUALITY", "GOLD")
133	    if min_faithfulness is None:
134	        try:
135	            min_faithfulness = float(os.getenv("PG_LTM_MIN_FAITHFULNESS", "0.9"))
136	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
137	            min_faithfulness = 0.9
138	
139	    manager = _get_chroma_manager()
140	    if manager is None:
141	        logger.warning("[cross_vector] ChromaDB not available; skipping promote_to_ltm")
142	        return 0
143	
144	    collection = _get_collection(manager)
145	    if collection is None:
146	        logger.warning("[cross_vector] Collection unavailable; skipping promote_to_ltm")
147	        return 0
148	
149	    min_rank = _TIER_RANK.get(min_quality, 3)
150	    promoted = 0
151	
152	    for piece in evidence_pieces:
153	        evidence_id = piece.get("evidence_id", "")
154	        if not evidence_id:
155	            continue
156	
157	        # Quality gate
158	        tier = piece.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
159	        tier_rank = _TIER_RANK.get(tier, 0)
160	        if tier_rank < min_rank:
161	            continue
162	
163	        # Faithfulness gate
164	        faithfulness = piece.get("faithfulness", 0.0)
165	        if faithfulness < min_faithfulness:
166	            continue
167	
168	        statement = piece.get("statement", "")
169	        if not statement:
170	            continue
171	
172	        # Build metadata (ChromaDB requires flat string/int/float values)
173	        source = piece.get("source", "")
174	        metadata = {
175	            "vector_id": vector_id,
176	            "quality_tier": tier,
177	            "faithfulness": float(faithfulness),
178	            "relevance_score": float(piece.get("relevance_score", 0.0)),
179	            "perspective": piece.get("perspective", ""),
180	            "source": source[:500] if source else "",
181	            "domain": _extract_domain(source),
182	        }
183	
184	        doc_id = f"ltm_{vector_id}_{evidence_id}"
185	
186	        try:
187	            collection.upsert(
188	                ids=[doc_id],
189	                documents=[statement],
190	                metadatas=[metadata],
191	            )
192	            promoted += 1
193	        except Exception as exc:
194	            logger.debug(
195	                "[cross_vector] Failed to upsert %s: %s",
196	                doc_id[:60], str(exc)[:200],
197	            )
198	
199	    if promoted > 0:
200	        logger.info(
201	            "[cross_vector] Promoted %d/%d evidence pieces to LTM-Global for %s",
202	            promoted, len(evidence_pieces), vector_id[:60],
203	        )
204	    return promoted
205	
206	
207	def query_ltm(query: str, max_results: int = 20) -> list[dict]:
208	    """Query ChromaDB LTM-Global for existing evidence on related topics.
209	
210	    Args:
211	        query: Natural-language query string.
212	        max_results: Maximum number of results (default 20).
213	
214	    Returns:
215	        List of dicts with keys: id, statement, source, quality_tier,
216	        faithfulness, relevance_score, vector_id, distance.
217	        Empty list if ChromaDB is unavailable.
218	    """
219	    if not query:
220	        return []
221	
222	    manager = _get_chroma_manager()
223	    if manager is None:
224	        logger.warning("[cross_vector] ChromaDB not available; query_ltm returns []")
225	        return []
226	
227	    collection = _get_collection(manager)
228	    if collection is None:
229	        logger.warning("[cross_vector] Collection unavailable; query_ltm returns []")
230	        return []
231	
232	    try:
233	        results = collection.query(
234	            query_texts=[query],
235	            n_results=max_results,
236	            include=["documents", "metadatas", "distances"],
237	        )
238	
239	        if not results or not results.get("ids") or not results["ids"][0]:
240	            return []
241	
242	        items = []

284	def get_ltm_stats() -> dict:
285	    """Get statistics for the LTM-Global collection.
286	
287	    Returns dict with keys:
288	        total_items (int), by_tier (dict[str, int]),
289	        top_domains (list[dict]), available (bool).
290	    """
291	    manager = _get_chroma_manager()
292	    if manager is None:
293	        return {
294	            "total_items": 0,
295	            "by_tier": {},
296	            "top_domains": [],
297	            "available": False,
298	        }
299	
300	    collection = _get_collection(manager)
301	    if collection is None:
302	        return {
303	            "total_items": 0,
304	            "by_tier": {},
305	            "top_domains": [],
306	            "available": False,
307	        }
308	
309	    try:
310	        total = collection.count()
311	
312	        if total == 0:
313	            return {
314	                "total_items": 0,
315	                "by_tier": {},
316	                "top_domains": [],
317	                "available": True,
318	            }
319	
320	        # Fetch all metadata for aggregation (capped at 10000 for safety)
321	        fetch_limit = min(total, 10000)
322	        all_items = collection.get(
323	            limit=fetch_limit,
324	            include=["metadatas"],
325	        )
326	
327	        by_tier: dict[str, int] = {}
328	        domain_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
329	
330	        if all_items and all_items.get("metadatas"):
331	            for meta in all_items["metadatas"]:
332	                if not meta:
333	                    continue
334	                tier = meta.get("quality_tier", "UNKNOWN")
335	                by_tier[tier] = by_tier.get(tier, 0) + 1
336	
337	                domain = meta.get("domain", "")
338	                if domain:
339	                    domain_counts[domain] = domain_counts.get(domain, 0) + 1
340	
341	        # Top 20 domains by count
342	        sorted_domains = sorted(
343	            domain_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True
344	        )[:20]
345	        top_domains = [
346	            {"domain": d, "count": c} for d, c in sorted_domains
347	        ]
348	
349	        return {
350	            "total_items": total,
351	            "by_tier": by_tier,
352	            "top_domains": top_domains,
353	            "available": True,
354	        }
355	
356	    except Exception as exc:
357	        logger.warning(
358	            "[cross_vector] get_ltm_stats failed: %s", str(exc)[:200],
359	        )
360	        return {
361	            "total_items": 0,
362	            "by_tier": {},
363	            "top_domains": [],
364	            "available": False,
365	        }

30	async def _ensure_table(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:
31	    """Create the evidence_memory table if it doesn't exist."""
32	    await db.execute("""
33	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS evidence_memory (
34	            evidence_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
35	            vector_id TEXT NOT NULL,
36	            cluster_id TEXT DEFAULT '',
37	            l0_summary TEXT DEFAULT '',
38	            l1_overview TEXT DEFAULT '',
39	            l2_full TEXT DEFAULT '{}',
40	            perspective TEXT DEFAULT '',
41	            quality_tier TEXT DEFAULT 'BRONZE',
42	            relevance_score REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
43	            created_at REAL NOT NULL
44	        )
45	    """)
46	    # TIER-3 Stage 4: Add section_assignments column (safe migration)
47	    try:
48	        await db.execute(
49	            "ALTER TABLE evidence_memory ADD COLUMN section_assignments TEXT DEFAULT ''"
50	        )
51	    except Exception:
52	        pass  # Column already exists
53	    await db.execute("""
54	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_evidence_vector
55	            ON evidence_memory (vector_id)
56	    """)
57	    await db.execute("""
58	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_evidence_vector_cluster
59	            ON evidence_memory (vector_id, cluster_id)
60	    """)
61	    await db.execute("""
62	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_evidence_perspective
63	            ON evidence_memory (vector_id, perspective)
64	    """)
65	    await db.execute("""
66	        CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_evidence_tier
67	            ON evidence_memory (vector_id, quality_tier)
68	    """)
69	    await db.commit()

72	async def store_evidence(
73	    evidence_id: str,
74	    vector_id: str,
75	    cluster_id: str,
76	    l0_summary: str,
77	    l1_overview: str,
78	    l2_json: dict,
79	    perspective: str,
80	    quality_tier: str,
81	    relevance_score: float,
82	) -> bool:
83	    """Store evidence at all three hierarchy levels.
84	
85	    Args:
86	        evidence_id: Unique evidence identifier (e.g., ev_abc123).
87	        vector_id: Parent vector identifier.
88	        cluster_id: Cluster this evidence belongs to.
89	        l0_summary: One-sentence claim (~100 tokens).
90	        l1_overview: Claim + context + quality (~500 tokens).
91	        l2_json: Full evidence piece as a dict.
92	        perspective: STORM perspective tag (e.g., 'public_health_expert').
93	        quality_tier: GOLD / SILVER / BRONZE.
94	        relevance_score: Float 0.0-1.0.
95	
96	    Returns:
97	        True on success.
98	    """
99	    if not evidence_id or not vector_id:
100	        return False
101	
102	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	    try:
104	        l2_text = json.dumps(l2_json, ensure_ascii=False)
105	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
106	            await _ensure_table(db)
107	            await db.execute(
108	                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO evidence_memory
109	                   (evidence_id, vector_id, cluster_id, l0_summary, l1_overview,
110	                    l2_full, perspective, quality_tier, relevance_score, created_at)
111	                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
112	                (
113	                    evidence_id,
114	                    vector_id,
115	                    cluster_id or "",
116	                    l0_summary or "",
117	                    l1_overview or "",
118	                    l2_text,
119	                    perspective or "",
120	                    quality_tier or "BRONZE",
121	                    relevance_score,
122	                    time.time(),
123	                ),
124	            )
125	            await db.commit()
126	        return True
127	    except Exception as exc:
128	        logger.warning(
129	            "[evidence_hierarchy] Write failed for %s: %s",
130	            evidence_id[:60], str(exc)[:200],
131	        )
132	        return False

1	"""Campaign knowledge-graph reuse read-path, FAIL-CLOSED (I-meta-002-q1d #948).
2	
3	The `VerifiedClaimGraphStore` was WRITE-ONLY — nothing read `query_related_claims` back into generation, so
4	the citation-snowball never reused a prior question's VERIFIED facts. This wires the read-path in, but the
5	reuse is MECHANICALLY fail-closed (Codex brief-gate iter-1 P1): a prior-VERIFIED claim is OMITTED before it
6	ever reaches a prompt UNLESS the CURRENT question's corpus independently supports it, judged by the SAME
7	content-overlap + decimal primitives the verified chokepoint (`strict_verify`) uses. A surviving claim is
8	anchored ONLY to the CURRENT evidence id that supports it — NEVER a prior evidence id, NEVER prior provenance.
9	
10	So reuse can only ever SURFACE a fact that is already present-and-supported in this question's corpus
11	(a cross-question relevance signal); it can never introduce an unsupported claim. The verified core
12	(multi_section generator + strict_verify) and the anti-poisoning store (only VERIFIED rows reusable) are
13	untouched. Default OFF (`PG_SWEEP_KG_REUSE`); read-only; no network; no model; NO SPEND.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import os
19	from typing import Any
20	
21	# Reuse the verified chokepoint's OWN primitives so the match-gate is identical to strict_verify's
22	# content rule (§9.1.3 checks (d) decimal-subset + (e) >= min content-word overlap).
23	from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import (
24	    _content_words,
25	    _decimals,
26	    _min_overlap_threshold,
27	)
28	
29	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "False", "no", "off", ""})
30	
31	
32	def kg_reuse_enabled() -> bool:
33	    """Default OFF — the read-path changes the analyst prompt; opt-in until a live smoke validates it.
34	    Normalize before comparison (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2) so FALSE/NO/OFF (any case) stay disabled."""
35	    return os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_KG_REUSE", "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
36	
37	
38	def match_prior_claims_to_current_corpus(
39	    prior_claim_texts: list[str],
40	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
41	) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
42	    """MECHANICAL fail-closed gate. Return one record per prior claim that is INDEPENDENTLY supported by
43	    the CURRENT corpus — `{"claim_text", "evidence_id"}` anchored to the matching CURRENT evidence id.
44	    A prior claim is kept iff some current evidence row shares >= min-overlap content words AND contains
45	    every decimal in the claim (mirroring strict_verify). Unmatched prior claims are OMITTED (never
46	    returned, so they never reach a prompt). NEVER returns a prior evidence id."""
47	    threshold = _min_overlap_threshold()
48	    out: list[dict[str, str]] = []
49	    seen: set[str] = set()
50	    for claim in prior_claim_texts or []:
51	        claim = (claim or "").strip()
52	        if not claim or claim in seen:
53	            continue
54	        claim_words = _content_words(claim)
55	        claim_decimals = _decimals(claim)
56	        for ev in evidence_rows or []:
57	            quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
58	            if not quote:
59	                continue
60	            if (len(claim_words & _content_words(quote)) >= threshold
61	                    and claim_decimals <= _decimals(quote)):
62	                seen.add(claim)
63	                out.append({"claim_text": claim, "evidence_id": str(ev.get("evidence_id", ""))})
64	                break  # first current-corpus support is enough
65	    return out
66	
67	
68	def gather_reuse_context(
69	    campaign_db_path: str,
70	    question: str,
71	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
72	    *,
73	    cap: int = 5,
74	) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
75	    """Open the campaign KG READ-ONLY, query prior-VERIFIED claims related to `question`, and return only
76	    those mechanically supported by the CURRENT corpus (capped). Fail-open: any store/read error returns
77	    [] (reuse is advisory; it must never abort or alter a run)."""
78	    if not kg_reuse_enabled():
79	        return []
80	    try:
81	        from src.polaris_graph.memory.verified_claim_graph import VerifiedClaimGraphStore
82	        # READ-ONLY open (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1): never create/migrate/write-lock/mutate the campaign
83	        # db. A missing/unreadable db raises → the outer except fail-opens to [].
84	        store = VerifiedClaimGraphStore(db_path=campaign_db_path, read_only=True)
85	        try:
86	            related = store.query_related_claims(question)
87	        finally:
88	            store.close()
89	        prior_texts = [r.claim_text for r in related if getattr(r, "claim_text", "")]
90	        matched = match_prior_claims_to_current_corpus(prior_texts, evidence_rows)
91	        return matched[:cap]
92	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — read-only advisory; never abort the run
93	        return []

1	"""
2	Session-scoped RAG over locally uploaded documents using ChromaDB.
3	
4	Each research session gets an isolated ChromaDB collection (``docs_{session_id}``)
5	that holds chunked and embedded document content.  All embedding is performed
6	locally via the POLARIS singleton EmbeddingService (sentence-transformers
7	all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dims) -- no external API calls.
8	
9	Persistence directory: PG_DOC_RAG_DIR env var (default: state/doc_rag).
10	
11	Usage:
12	    rag = LocalDocumentRAG("session_abc123")
13	    chunk_count = await rag.ingest_document(doc_id, content, metadata)
14	    results = await rag.query("What contaminants are regulated?", k=10)
15	    await rag.cleanup()
16	"""
17	
18	import asyncio
19	import hashlib
20	import logging
21	import os
22	import re
23	import time
24	from pathlib import Path
25	from typing import Optional
26	
27	from dotenv import load_dotenv
28	
29	load_dotenv()
30	
31	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# Configuration (LAW VI: all from env vars)
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	DOC_RAG_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_DOC_RAG_DIR", "state/doc_rag"))
38	CHUNK_SIZE_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_DOC_RAG_CHUNK_SIZE", "512"))
39	CHUNK_OVERLAP_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_DOC_RAG_CHUNK_OVERLAP", "50"))
40	COLLECTION_PREFIX = "docs_"
41	
42	# Approximate characters per token for whitespace-split tokenisation.
43	# sentence-transformers uses a WordPiece tokenizer; 1 token ~ 4 chars is a
44	# reasonable approximation for English prose.
45	CHARS_PER_TOKEN = int(os.getenv("PG_DOC_RAG_CHARS_PER_TOKEN", "4"))
46	
47	# Maximum batch size for ChromaDB upsert to avoid memory pressure.
48	UPSERT_BATCH_SIZE = int(os.getenv("PG_DOC_RAG_UPSERT_BATCH", "256"))
49	
50	
51	class LocalDocumentRAG:
52	    """Session-scoped retrieval-augmented generation over uploaded documents.
53	
54	    Creates an isolated ChromaDB collection per session so that document
55	    embeddings do not leak across sessions.
56	    """
57	
58	    def __init__(self, session_id: str) -> None:
59	        if not session_id or not session_id.strip():
60	            raise ValueError("session_id must be a non-empty string.")
61	
62	        self._session_id = session_id
63	        self._collection_name = f"{COLLECTION_PREFIX}{session_id}"
64	        self._client = None
65	        self._collection = None
66	        self._embedding_fn = None
67	
68	        logger.info(
69	            "[local_document_rag] Initialised for session=%s (collection=%s)",
70	            session_id,
71	            self._collection_name,
72	        )
73	
74	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
75	    # Lazy initialisation (avoid import-time side effects)
76	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
77	
78	    def _ensure_client(self) -> None:
79	        """Create the PersistentClient and collection on first access."""
80	        if self._client is not None:
81	            return
82	
83	        try:
84	            import chromadb
85	            from chromadb.config import Settings
86	        except ImportError as exc:
87	            raise RuntimeError(
88	                "chromadb is required for LocalDocumentRAG. "
89	                "Install with: pip install chromadb"
90	            ) from exc
91	
92	        persist_path = DOC_RAG_DIR.resolve()
93	        persist_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
94	
95	        self._client = chromadb.PersistentClient(
96	            path=str(persist_path),
97	            settings=Settings(
98	                anonymized_telemetry=False,
99	                allow_reset=False,
100	            ),
101	        )
102	
103	        # Use the POLARIS singleton embedding function so that embeddings
104	        # are consistent with the rest of the pipeline (all-MiniLM-L6-v2).
105	        try:
106	            from src.utils.embedding_service import get_chromadb_embedding_function
107	            self._embedding_fn = get_chromadb_embedding_function()
108	        except Exception as exc:
109	            logger.warning(
110	                "[local_document_rag] Failed to load POLARIS embedding function, "
111	                "falling back to ChromaDB default: %s",
112	                str(exc)[:200],
113	            )
114	            self._embedding_fn = None
115	
116	        kwargs = {"name": self._collection_name}
117	        if self._embedding_fn is not None:
118	            kwargs["embedding_function"] = self._embedding_fn
119	
120	        self._collection = self._client.get_or_create_collection(**kwargs)
121	
122	        logger.info(
123	            "[local_document_rag] ChromaDB client ready at %s "
124	            "(collection=%s, count=%d)",
125	            persist_path,
126	            self._collection_name,
127	            self._collection.count(),
128	        )
129	
130	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
131	    # Public API
132	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
133	
134	    async def ingest_document(
135	        self,
136	        doc_id: str,
137	        content: str,
138	        metadata: Optional[dict] = None,
139	    ) -> int:
140	        """Chunk *content*, embed, and upsert into the session collection.
141	
142	        Args:
143	            doc_id:   Unique identifier for the document.
144	            content:  Full extracted plain-text of the document.
145	            metadata: Optional document-level metadata to attach to every
146	                      chunk (e.g. filename, author, pages).
147	
148	        Returns:
149	            Number of chunks upserted.
150	
151	        Raises:
152	            ValueError: If doc_id or content is empty.
153	        """
154	        if not doc_id or not doc_id.strip():
155	            raise ValueError("doc_id must be a non-empty string.")
156	        if not content or not content.strip():
157	            raise ValueError(
158	                f"content is empty for doc_id={doc_id}. "
159	                "Cannot ingest an empty document."
160	            )
161	
162	        self._ensure_client()
163	        start_ts = time.monotonic()
164	
165	        chunks = _chunk_text(
166	            content,
167	            chunk_size_tokens=CHUNK_SIZE_TOKENS,
168	            overlap_tokens=CHUNK_OVERLAP_TOKENS,
169	        )
170	
171	        if not chunks:
172	            logger.warning(
173	                "[local_document_rag] Chunking produced 0 chunks for "
174	                "doc_id=%s (%d chars).",
175	                doc_id, len(content),
176	            )
177	            return 0
178	
179	        # Build ids, documents, metadatas for ChromaDB upsert
180	        ids: list[str] = []
181	        documents: list[str] = []
182	        metadatas: list[dict] = []
183	        base_meta = metadata or {}
184	
185	        for idx, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
186	            chunk_id = _make_chunk_id(doc_id, idx)
187	            chunk_meta = {
188	                "doc_id": doc_id,
189	                "chunk_index": idx,
190	                "chunk_count": len(chunks),
191	                "char_start": chunk["char_start"],
192	                "char_end": chunk["char_end"],
193	            }
194	            # Merge document-level metadata (only scalar values for ChromaDB)
195	            for key, val in base_meta.items():
196	                if isinstance(val, (str, int, float, bool)):
197	                    chunk_meta[key] = val
198	
199	            ids.append(chunk_id)
200	            documents.append(chunk["text"])
201	            metadatas.append(chunk_meta)
202	
203	        # Upsert in batches to avoid memory pressure
204	        total_upserted = 0
205	        for batch_start in range(0, len(ids), UPSERT_BATCH_SIZE):
206	            batch_end = batch_start + UPSERT_BATCH_SIZE
207	            self._collection.upsert(
208	                ids=ids[batch_start:batch_end],
209	                documents=documents[batch_start:batch_end],
210	                metadatas=metadatas[batch_start:batch_end],
211	            )
212	            total_upserted += len(ids[batch_start:batch_end])
213	
214	            # Yield to the event loop between batches
215	            if batch_end < len(ids):
216	                await asyncio.sleep(0)
217	
218	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_ts
219	        logger.info(
220	            "[local_document_rag] Ingested doc_id=%s -> %d chunks in %.2fs "
221	            "(collection=%s, total=%d)",
222	            doc_id,
223	            total_upserted,
224	            elapsed,
225	            self._collection_name,
226	            self._collection.count(),
227	        )
228	        return total_upserted
229	
230	    async def query(
231	        self,
232	        query: str,
233	        k: int = 10,
234	        where_filter: Optional[dict] = None,
235	    ) -> list[dict]:
236	        """Retrieve the top-*k* most relevant chunks for *query*.
237	
238	        Args:
239	            query:        Natural language query string.
240	            k:            Number of results to return (default 10).
241	            where_filter: Optional ChromaDB where clause to filter by
242	                          metadata (e.g. ``{"doc_id": "abc123"}``).
243	
244	        Returns:
245	            List of dicts, each containing:
246	                chunk_id  (str):   The unique chunk identifier.
247	                text      (str):   The chunk content.
248	                metadata  (dict):  Attached metadata.
249	                distance  (float): Embedding distance (lower = more similar).
250	        """
251	        if not query or not query.strip():
252	            logger.warning("[local_document_rag] Empty query, returning [].")
253	            return []
254	
255	        self._ensure_client()
256	
257	        collection_count = self._collection.count()
258	        if collection_count == 0:
259	            logger.info(
260	                "[local_document_rag] Collection %s is empty, returning [].",
261	                self._collection_name,
262	            )
263	            return []
264	
265	        effective_k = min(k, collection_count)
266	
267	        query_kwargs = {
268	            "query_texts": [query],
269	            "n_results": effective_k,
270	        }
271	        if where_filter:
272	            query_kwargs["where"] = where_filter
273	
274	        try:
275	            results = self._collection.query(**query_kwargs)
276	        except Exception as exc:
277	            logger.error(
278	                "[local_document_rag] Query failed on collection=%s: %s",
279	                self._collection_name,
280	                str(exc)[:300],
281	            )
282	            raise
283	
284	        # Unpack ChromaDB results into a flat list
285	        output: list[dict] = []
286	        if not results or not results.get("ids"):
287	            return output
288	
289	        result_ids = results["ids"][0]
290	        result_docs = results["documents"][0] if results.get("documents") else []
291	        result_metas = results["metadatas"][0] if results.get("metadatas") else []
292	        result_dists = results["distances"][0] if results.get("distances") else []
293	
294	        for i, chunk_id in enumerate(result_ids):
295	            output.append({
296	                "chunk_id": chunk_id,
297	                "text": result_docs[i] if i < len(result_docs) else "",
298	                "metadata": result_metas[i] if i < len(result_metas) else {},
299	                "distance": result_dists[i] if i < len(result_dists) else 0.0,
300	            })
301	
302	        logger.debug(
303	            "[local_document_rag] Query returned %d results from %s "
304	            "(k=%d, collection_count=%d).",
305	            len(output),
306	            self._collection_name,
307	            effective_k,
308	            collection_count,
309	        )
310	        return output
311	
312	    async def cleanup(self) -> None:
313	        """Delete the session-specific collection and release resources."""
314	        if self._client is None:
315	            logger.debug(
316	                "[local_document_rag] cleanup() called but client was "
317	                "never initialised for session=%s.",
318	                self._session_id,
319	            )
320	            return
321	
322	        try:
323	            self._client.delete_collection(name=self._collection_name)
324	            logger.info(
325	                "[local_document_rag] Deleted collection %s for session=%s.",
326	                self._collection_name,
327	                self._session_id,
328	            )
329	        except Exception as exc:
330	            logger.warning(
331	                "[local_document_rag] Failed to delete collection %s: %s",
332	                self._collection_name,
333	                str(exc)[:200],
334	            )
335	
336	        self._collection = None
337	        self._client = None
338	
339	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
340	    # Convenience properties
341	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
342	
343	    @property
344	    def session_id(self) -> str:
345	        """Return the session identifier."""
346	        return self._session_id
347	
348	    @property
349	    def collection_name(self) -> str:
350	        """Return the underlying ChromaDB collection name."""
351	        return self._collection_name
352	
353	    @property
354	    def chunk_count(self) -> int:
355	        """Return the total number of chunks in the collection."""
356	        if self._collection is None:
357	            return 0
358	        return self._collection.count()
359	
360	    def get_document_ids(self) -> list[str]:
361	        """Return distinct doc_ids stored in this session collection."""
362	        if self._collection is None:
363	            return []
364	
365	        try:
366	            all_meta = self._collection.get(include=["metadatas"])
367	            metadatas = all_meta.get("metadatas") or []
368	            doc_ids = sorted({
369	                m.get("doc_id", "")
370	                for m in metadatas
371	                if m.get("doc_id")
372	            })
373	            return doc_ids
374	        except Exception as exc:
375	            logger.warning(
376	                "[local_document_rag] Failed to list doc_ids: %s",
377	                str(exc)[:200],
378	            )
379	            return []
380	
381	
382	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
383	# Chunking utilities
384	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
385	
386	def _chunk_text(
387	    text: str,
388	    chunk_size_tokens: int = CHUNK_SIZE_TOKENS,
389	    overlap_tokens: int = CHUNK_OVERLAP_TOKENS,
390	) -> list[dict]:
391	    """Split *text* into overlapping chunks of approximately *chunk_size_tokens*.
392	
393	    Each chunk dict contains:
394	        text       (str): The chunk content.
395	        char_start (int): Start character offset in the original text.
396	        char_end   (int): End character offset in the original text.
397	
398	    The chunker splits on paragraph boundaries first, then on sentence
399	    boundaries, to preserve semantic coherence.  Token counts are
400	    approximated using ``CHARS_PER_TOKEN``.
401	    """
402	    if not text or not text.strip():
403	        return []
404	
405	    chunk_size_chars = chunk_size_tokens * CHARS_PER_TOKEN
406	    overlap_chars = overlap_tokens * CHARS_PER_TOKEN
407	
408	    # Split into paragraphs (double newline or more)
409	    paragraphs = re.split(r"\n{2,}", text)
410	
411	    chunks: list[dict] = []
412	    current_text = ""
413	    current_start = 0
414	    cursor = 0  # tracks character position in original text
415	
416	    for para in paragraphs:
417	        para = para.strip()
418	        if not para:
419	            # Account for the split characters
420	            cursor += 2  # approximate for \n\n
421	            continue
422	
423	        # Find actual position of this paragraph in original text
424	        para_start = text.find(para, cursor)
425	        if para_start == -1:
426	            para_start = cursor
427	        para_end = para_start + len(para)
428	
429	        # Would adding this paragraph exceed the chunk size?
430	        tentative = (current_text + "\n\n" + para).strip() if current_text else para
431	        if len(tentative) > chunk_size_chars and current_text:
432	            # Flush current chunk
433	            chunks.append({
434	                "text": current_text,
435	                "char_start": current_start,
436	                "char_end": current_start + len(current_text),
437	            })
438	
439	            # Start new chunk with overlap
440	            if overlap_chars > 0 and len(current_text) > overlap_chars:
441	                overlap_text = current_text[-overlap_chars:]
442	                # Start from a sentence boundary within the overlap if possible
443	                sent_break = _find_sentence_break(overlap_text)
444	                if sent_break > 0:
445	                    overlap_text = overlap_text[sent_break:].strip()
446	                current_text = overlap_text + "\n\n" + para
447	                current_start = para_start - len(overlap_text)
448	            else:
449	                current_text = para
450	                current_start = para_start
451	        else:
452	            if not current_text:
453	                current_start = para_start
454	            current_text = tentative
455	
456	        cursor = para_end
457	
458	    # Flush remaining
459	    if current_text.strip():
460	        chunks.append({
461	            "text": current_text.strip(),
462	            "char_start": current_start,
463	            "char_end": current_start + len(current_text.strip()),
464	        })
465	
466	    # Handle oversized chunks by splitting on sentence boundaries
467	    final_chunks: list[dict] = []
468	    for chunk in chunks:
469	        if len(chunk["text"]) <= chunk_size_chars * 1.5:
470	            final_chunks.append(chunk)
471	        else:
472	            sub_chunks = _split_oversized_chunk(
473	                chunk["text"],
474	                chunk["char_start"],
475	                chunk_size_chars,
476	                overlap_chars,
477	            )
478	            final_chunks.extend(sub_chunks)
479	
480	    return final_chunks
481	
482	
483	def _split_oversized_chunk(
484	    text: str,
485	    base_offset: int,
486	    chunk_size_chars: int,
487	    overlap_chars: int,
488	) -> list[dict]:
489	    """Split an oversized chunk on sentence boundaries."""
490	    sentences = _split_sentences(text)
491	    chunks: list[dict] = []
492	    current_text = ""
493	    current_start = base_offset
494	    cursor = 0
495	
496	    for sentence in sentences:
497	        sentence = sentence.strip()
498	        if not sentence:
499	            continue
500	
501	        tentative = (current_text + " " + sentence).strip() if current_text else sentence
502	        if len(tentative) > chunk_size_chars and current_text:
503	            chunks.append({
504	                "text": current_text,
505	                "char_start": current_start,
506	                "char_end": current_start + len(current_text),
507	            })
508	
509	            if overlap_chars > 0 and len(current_text) > overlap_chars:
510	                overlap_text = current_text[-overlap_chars:]
511	                current_text = overlap_text + " " + sentence
512	            else:
513	                current_text = sentence
514	            current_start = base_offset + cursor
515	        else:
516	            if not current_text:
517	                current_start = base_offset + cursor
518	            current_text = tentative
519	
520	        cursor += len(sentence) + 1  # +1 for space/newline
521	
522	    if current_text.strip():
523	        chunks.append({
524	            "text": current_text.strip(),
525	            "char_start": current_start,
526	            "char_end": current_start + len(current_text.strip()),
527	        })
528	
529	    return chunks
530	
531	
532	def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
533	    """Split text into sentences using pysbd or regex fallback."""
534	    try:
535	        import pysbd
536	        segmenter = pysbd.Segmenter(language="en", clean=False)
537	        return segmenter.segment(text)
538	    except ImportError:
539	        pass
540	
541	    # Regex fallback: split after sentence-ending punctuation followed by
542	    # a space and an uppercase letter.
543	    return re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z])", text)
544	
545	
546	def _find_sentence_break(text: str) -> int:
547	    """Find the first sentence break position in *text*.
548	
549	    Returns the index right after the sentence-ending punctuation,
550	    or 0 if no break is found.
551	    """
552	    match = re.search(r"[.!?]\s+", text)
553	    if match:
554	        return match.end()
555	    return 0
556	
557	
558	def _make_chunk_id(doc_id: str, chunk_index: int) -> str:
559	    """Deterministic chunk identifier: ``{doc_id}_c{chunk_index:04d}``."""
560	    return f"{doc_id}_c{chunk_index:04d}"

1	"""
2	Persistent search result cache backed by SQLite.
3	
4	Caches search API results (Serper, S2, Exa, DDG) to avoid duplicate queries.
5	TTL-based expiry (default 24 hours for freshness).
6	"""
7	
8	import hashlib
9	import json
10	import logging
11	import os
12	import time
13	from pathlib import Path
14	from typing import Optional
15	
16	import aiosqlite
17	from dotenv import load_dotenv
18	
19	load_dotenv()
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	CACHE_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_CACHE_DIR", "state"))
24	CACHE_DB = CACHE_DIR / "pg_search_cache.sqlite"
25	DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS = int(os.getenv("PG_SEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS", "24"))
26	
27	
28	def _query_hash(query: str, search_type: str = "") -> str:
29	    """Generate a consistent hash for a query + type pair."""
30	    key = f"{search_type}:{query}".lower().strip()
31	    return hashlib.sha256(key.encode()).hexdigest()[:32]
32	
33	
34	async def _ensure_table(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:
35	    """Create the cache table if it doesn't exist."""
36	    await db.execute("""
37	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS search_cache (
38	            query_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
39	            query_text TEXT NOT NULL,
40	            results_json TEXT NOT NULL,
41	            search_type TEXT DEFAULT '',
42	            result_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
43	            cached_at REAL NOT NULL,
44	            ttl_hours INTEGER DEFAULT 24
45	        )
46	    """)
47	    await db.commit()
48	
49	
50	async def get_cached_results(
51	    query: str,
52	    search_type: str = "",
53	) -> Optional[list[dict]]:
54	    """Retrieve cached search results.
55	
56	    Returns list of result dicts, or None if not cached/expired.
57	    """
58	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
59	    qhash = _query_hash(query, search_type)
60	    try:
61	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
62	            await _ensure_table(db)
63	            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row
64	            cursor = await db.execute(
65	                "SELECT * FROM search_cache WHERE query_hash = ?", (qhash,)
66	            )
67	            row = await cursor.fetchone()
68	            if row is None:
69	                return None
70	
71	            # Check TTL
72	            cached_at = row["cached_at"]
73	            ttl_hours = row["ttl_hours"] or DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS
74	            age_hours = (time.time() - cached_at) / 3600
75	            if age_hours > ttl_hours:
76	                return None
77	
78	            return json.loads(row["results_json"])
79	    except Exception as exc:
80	        logger.warning(
81	            "[search_cache] Read failed for '%s': %s",
82	            query[:50], str(exc)[:200],
83	        )
84	        return None
85	
86	
87	async def cache_results(
88	    query: str,
89	    results: list[dict],
90	    search_type: str = "",
91	    ttl_hours: int = DEFAULT_TTL_HOURS,
92	) -> bool:
93	    """Store search results in cache."""
94	    if not query or not results:
95	        return False
96	
97	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
98	    qhash = _query_hash(query, search_type)
99	    try:
100	        results_json = json.dumps(results, ensure_ascii=False)
101	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
102	            await _ensure_table(db)
103	            await db.execute(
104	                """INSERT OR REPLACE INTO search_cache
105	                   (query_hash, query_text, results_json, search_type,
106	                    result_count, cached_at, ttl_hours)
107	                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
108	                (qhash, query, results_json, search_type,
109	                 len(results), time.time(), ttl_hours),
110	            )
111	            await db.commit()
112	        return True
113	    except Exception as exc:
114	        logger.warning(
115	            "[search_cache] Write failed for '%s': %s",
116	            query[:50], str(exc)[:200],
117	        )
118	        return False
119	
120	
121	async def get_cache_stats() -> dict:
122	    """Get cache statistics."""
123	    CACHE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
124	    try:
125	        async with aiosqlite.connect(str(CACHE_DB)) as db:
126	            await _ensure_table(db)
127	            cursor = await db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM search_cache")
128	            total = (await cursor.fetchone())[0]
129	
130	            cursor = await db.execute("SELECT SUM(result_count) FROM search_cache")
131	            total_results = (await cursor.fetchone())[0] or 0
132	
133	            cursor = await db.execute(
134	                "SELECT search_type, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM search_cache GROUP BY search_type"
135	            )
136	            by_type = {row[0]: row[1] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}
137	
138	            return {
139	                "total_queries": total,
140	                "total_results_cached": total_results,
141	                "by_type": by_type,
142	            }
143	    except Exception as exc:
144	        logger.warning("[search_cache] Stats failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
145	        return {"total_queries": 0, "total_results_cached": 0, "by_type": {}}
