513	
514	def _topic_applies(
515	    topic: ChecklistTopic,
516	    research_question: str,
517	    evidence_blob: str,
518	) -> tuple[bool, str]:
519	    """Return ``(applies, disclosure_note)`` for a checklist topic.
520	
521	    BUG-7 (I-arch-006, #1262) — drive a drug/intervention-pharmacology topic's
522	    applicability from a ROBUST detector, not the routing label or a raw
523	    substring.
524	
525	    Non-drug topics (``requires_drug_intervention`` unset) keep the EXACT
526	    pre-BUG-7 `applies_if` substring semantics (question OR evidence pool), so
527	    their applicability is byte-identical.
528	
529	    A topic flagged ``requires_drug_intervention: true`` applies iff the QUESTION
530	    is actually about a drug/intervention. "About a drug/intervention" is decided
531	    by EITHER:
532	      * the scope-gate recognizer (`_intervention_present`) finding a specific
533	        drug name / INN-stem in the question, OR
534	      * one of the topic's own `applies_if` CLASS anchors (e.g. "glp-1",
535	        "incretin") appearing in the QUESTION — these are intervention-class
536	        signals, not incidental evidence text.
537	    The question-detector deliberately consults the QUESTION only: a drug word that
538	    merely appears in retrieved EVIDENCE for a non-drug question must not
539	    QUESTION-flip a pharmacology topic on (the BUG-7 false-positive path).
540	
541	    U23 (I-deepfix-001) — CORPUS FALLBACK. When the question yields a CONFIDENT
542	    negative (recognizer ran, no intervention, no class anchor) and
543	    ``PG_COMPLETENESS_CORPUS_INTERVENTION`` is on (default), the same recognizer is
544	    applied to the CORPUS. A corpus hit makes NON-critical drug topics applicable so
545	    a study whose intervention is named only in evidence (e.g. an EDTA chelation RCT
546	    for a metal-ions/CVD question) is MEASURED instead of vacuously all-non-applicable.
547	    The CRITICAL contraindications topic is kept on the disclose path for a
548	    corpus-only hit (weaker signal than the question) so no spurious
549	    ``abort_critical_topic_uncovered`` HOLD is reintroduced.
550	
551	    Once the question is confirmed to be about a drug/intervention, the topic's
552	    NORMAL `applies_if` filter still applies (so the GLP-1-class topic stays gated
553	    to GLP-1 questions and does not fire for metformin). For a topic with no
554	    `applies_if` (the CRITICAL `contraindications` topic), drug-presence is the
555	    sole gate.
556	
557	    FAIL-CLOSED / DISCLOSE ON AMBIGUITY: if the recognizer cannot be consulted
558	    (import/config error), a CRITICAL ``requires_drug_intervention`` topic defaults
559	    to applies=True with a disclosure note — never silently mark a critical safety
560	    topic non-applicable. A non-critical topic falls back to the legacy substring
561	    match (adequacy elsewhere unchanged).
562	
563	    Faithfulness: applicability only refines the completeness DENOMINATOR; it
564	    never touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding, never drops a
565	    verified claim, and errs toward keeping a critical safety topic active.
566	    """
567	    legacy = _legacy_applies_if(topic, research_question, evidence_blob)
568	
569	    # Non-drug topic, or kill-switch OFF -> exact pre-BUG-7 behaviour.
570	    if not topic.requires_drug_intervention or not _drug_detector_enabled():
571	        return legacy, ""
572	
573	    q_lower = (research_question or "").lower()
574	    class_anchor_in_question = any(
575	        term in q_lower for term in topic.applies_if
576	    )
577	    detected, ambiguous = _intervention_detected_or_ambiguous(research_question)
578	
579	    if ambiguous:
580	        # Recognizer unavailable: we cannot confidently conclude "no drug".
581	        if topic.critical:
582	            # Fail-closed: keep the critical safety topic active + disclose.
583	            return True, (
584	                f"applicability of critical topic {topic.id!r} could not be "
585	                f"determined (intervention recognizer unavailable); kept "
586	                f"applicable fail-closed per BUG-7 (#1262)"
587	            )
588	        # Non-critical: fall back to the legacy substring match (no change to
589	        # adequacy on ambiguity).
590	        return legacy, ""
591	
592	    drug_or_intervention_present = detected or class_anchor_in_question
593	
594	    if drug_or_intervention_present:
595	        # The question IS about a drug/intervention. Apply the topic's normal
596	        # `applies_if` filter so class-specific topics stay gated to their class.
597	        return legacy, ""
598	
599	    # U23 (I-deepfix-001): the QUESTION named no intervention (confident negative,
600	    # not ambiguity). Fall back to recognising an intervention in the CORPUS via the
601	    # SAME recognizer, so a study whose intervention is named only in retrieved
602	    # evidence (e.g. an EDTA chelation RCT for a metal-ions/CVD question) still gets
603	    # its drug/intervention topics MEASURED instead of a vacuous all-non-applicable.
604	    corpus_token = (
605	        _corpus_intervention_present(evidence_blob)
606	        if _corpus_intervention_enabled()
607	        else ""
608	    )
609	    if corpus_token:
610	        if topic.critical:
611	            # Corpus-only detection for the CRITICAL topic: DISCLOSE, do NOT force
612	            # the topic applicable. Corpus recognition is weaker than question
613	            # recognition (BUG-7), so surface the corpus intervention for manual
614	            # contraindications review rather than risk a spurious
615	            # abort_critical_topic_uncovered HOLD.
616	            return False, (
617	                f"critical topic {topic.id!r}: an intervention ({corpus_token!r}) is "
618	                f"present in the retrieved corpus but not named in the question; "
619	                f"contraindications coverage was NOT force-applied (disclose-not-hold "
620	                f"per BUG-7). Verify contraindications coverage manually "
621	                f"(U23, I-deepfix-001)."
622	            )
623	        # Non-critical drug topic: an intervention is present in the corpus, so
624	        # measure this topic against it (normal `applies_if` filter still applies —
625	        # a class-specific topic stays gated to its class terms).
626	        return legacy, ""
627	
628	    # Confident negative: no intervention recognised in the question OR the corpus
629	    # (and no class anchor). (Reached only when `drug_or_intervention_present` is
630	    # False and the corpus fallback found nothing — both preceding branches returned.)
631	    if topic.critical:
632	        # Codex P1 (#1262): the recognizer (the SAME one the scope gate uses)
633	        # has incomplete brand/trade-name coverage, so a "confident no-drug" can
634	        # be a MISS on a real drug question — silently dropping a CRITICAL
635	        # contraindications topic would disable `abort_critical_topic_uncovered`
636	        # (a clinical-safety failure). A keyword "drug signal" heuristic to
637	        # auto-fail-closed proved a false-positive MINEFIELD (negation:
638	        # "non-pharmacological", "medication-free"; polysemy: "capsule endoscopy",
639	        # "monoclonal gammopathy"), and over-firing it would SPURIOUSLY HOLD a
640	        # non-drug report. Per Codex's explicit "fail-closed OR DISCLOSE" guidance
641	        # AND the operator's disclose-don't-hold directive, we take the DISCLOSE
642	        # path: keep applies=False (a non-drug report is NEVER spuriously held)
643	        # but ALWAYS emit a disclosure note, so the decision is auditable and the
644	        # skipped safety check is SURFACED FOR REVIEW rather than vanishing
645	        # silently — if this is in fact a drug question whose brand the recognizer
646	        # missed, the gap is disclosed in the report, not hidden.
647	        return False, (
648	            f"critical topic {topic.id!r}: marked non-applicable — the drug/"
649	            f"intervention recognizer found no known intervention in the question. "
650	            f"DISCLOSED (not silent): if this is a drug question with an "
651	            f"unrecognized brand/trade name, verify contraindications coverage "
652	            f"manually (Codex P1, #1262)."
653	        )
654	    # Non-critical confident negative: a drug-pharmacology topic does not apply
655	    # (the BUG-7 fix); a clean "no drug" is not ambiguity, no disclosure noise.
656	    return False, ""
657	
658	
659	def _compile_keyword_re(keywords: list[str]) -> re.Pattern:
660	    if not keywords:
661	        return re.compile(r"(?!x)x")  # never matches
662	    parts = [re.escape(k) for k in keywords]
663	    return re.compile(r"|".join(parts), re.IGNORECASE)
664	
665	
666	def check_completeness(
667	    *,
668	    domain: str,
669	    research_question: str,
670	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
671	    min_hits_to_cover: int = 1,
672	    drug_or_topic_hint: str = "",
673	    coverage_text: str | None = None,
674	) -> CompletenessReport:
675	    """Check evidence rows against the domain's completeness checklist.
676	
677	    Args:
678	        domain: scope-template domain name.
679	        research_question: the user's query (drives the `applies_if` filter and,
680	            for `requires_drug_intervention` topics, the BUG-7 robust
681	            drug/intervention applicability gate — see `_topic_applies`).
682	        evidence_rows: evidence dicts with 'direct_quote' / 'statement'.
683	        min_hits_to_cover: min # of evidence rows that must match at
684	            least one keyword for a topic to be "covered" (default 1).
685	        drug_or_topic_hint: optional {drug} substitution for expand_queries
686	            (otherwise uses first significant noun from the question).
687	        coverage_text: FINDING #6b (#1344). Optional ACTUAL rendered / verified
688	            report prose. When provided (and PG_COMPLETENESS_COVERAGE_AGAINST_OUTPUT
689	            is on, the default), a topic is "covered" only if its keywords appear IN
690	            THE PRESENTED REPORT — so an aspect the report does not actually cover is
691	            honestly reported as a GAP (uncovered), never falsely complete from a
692	            corpus keyword hit. `applies_if` still uses `evidence_rows`. When None
693	            (the retrieval-time gap-detection call) coverage is measured against the
694	            evidence corpus exactly as before — byte-identical.
695	
696	    Returns CompletenessReport with per-topic coverage + expand_queries
697	    for uncovered topics.
698	    """
699	    # FINDING #6b: coverage measured against the OUTPUT (rendered/verified prose) when
700	    # a `coverage_text` is supplied and the gate is on; else the legacy corpus-based
701	    # per-evidence-row hit count. `applies_if` always uses the evidence corpus.
702	    _use_output_coverage = (
703	        coverage_text is not None and _coverage_against_output_enabled()
704	    )
705	    _coverage_blob = str(coverage_text or "") if _use_output_coverage else None
706	    # BUG-20 (I-arch-011): route to a question-matched sub-domain checklist
707	    # (e.g. a Parkinson's/DBS question -> clinical_neuro_device) instead of the
708	    # fixed drug-efficacy clinical template. No-match -> domain unchanged.
709	    resolved_domain = _route_checklist_domain(domain, research_question)
710	    topics = load_checklist(resolved_domain)
711	    if not topics:
712	        return CompletenessReport(domain=resolved_domain, notes=["no_checklist_loaded"])
713	
714	    # Build an all-evidence text blob for applies_if checks
715	    evidence_blob = " ".join(
716	        (ev.get("direct_quote") or "") + " " + (ev.get("statement") or "")
717	        for ev in evidence_rows
718	    )
719	
720	    # Pick a substitution token for {drug}/{topic}/{company}/{product}
721	    # placeholders in expand_queries. Use explicit hint if given, else
722	    # attempt to pull a drug name or the first significant noun from
723	    # the research_question.
724	    token = (drug_or_topic_hint or "").strip()
725	    if not token:
726	        try:
727	            from src.polaris_graph.nodes.scope_gate import _DRUG_NAME_RE
728	            m = _DRUG_NAME_RE.search(research_question)
729	            if m:
730	                token = m.group(1)
731	        except Exception:
732	            pass
733	    if not token:
734	        # First capitalized or salient noun phrase, naive fallback
735	        m = re.search(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z\-]{3,})\b", research_question)
736	        if m:
737	            token = m.group(1)
738	    if not token:
739	        token = "topic"
740	
741	    coverages: list[TopicCoverage] = []
742	    all_expand_queries: list[str] = []
743	    # BUG-7 (I-arch-006, #1262): disclosure notes for any critical drug topic whose
744	    # applicability was decided by the recognizer — (a) fail-closed (kept applicable)
745	    # when the recognizer was UNAVAILABLE / ambiguous, or (b) pure-disclose (kept
746	    # NON-applicable but surfaced for review) on a recognizer confident-negative.
747	    applicability_disclosures: list[str] = []
748	
749	    for topic in topics:
750	        applies, disclosure = _topic_applies(
751	            topic, research_question, evidence_blob
752	        )
753	        if disclosure:
754	            applicability_disclosures.append(disclosure)
755	        if not applies:
756	            coverages.append(TopicCoverage(
757	                topic=topic, applies=False,
758	                covered=False, hits=0,
759	            ))
760	            continue
761	
762	        kw_re = _compile_keyword_re(topic.keywords)
763	        hits = 0
764	        matched: list[str] = []
765	        if _use_output_coverage:
766	            # FINDING #6b (#1344): "covered" iff a topic keyword appears in the ACTUAL
767	            # rendered/verified report prose, not merely somewhere in the retrieved
768	            # corpus. A topic mentioned only in dropped/unrendered evidence is honestly
769	            # reported UNCOVERED. Presence-based (one hit is enough at the default
770	            # min_hits_to_cover=1); no source is dropped and no faithfulness gate moves.
771	            for m in kw_re.finditer(_coverage_blob or ""):
772	                hits += 1
773	                if m.group(0).lower() not in matched:
774	                    matched.append(m.group(0).lower())
775	        else:
776	            for ev in evidence_rows:
777	                quote = (
778	                    (ev.get("direct_quote") or "")
779	                    + " "
780	                    + (ev.get("statement") or "")
781	                )
782	                m = kw_re.search(quote)
783	                if m:
784	                    hits += 1
785	                    if m.group(0).lower() not in matched:
786	                        matched.append(m.group(0).lower())
787	
788	        covered = hits >= min_hits_to_cover
789	        coverages.append(TopicCoverage(
790	            topic=topic, applies=True, covered=covered,
791	            hits=hits, matched_keywords=matched,
792	        ))
793	
794	        if not covered:
795	            # Substitute placeholders
796	            for q in topic.expand_queries:
797	                q_sub = (
798	                    q.replace("{drug}", token)
799	                     .replace("{topic}", token)
800	                     .replace("{company}", token)
801	                     .replace("{product}", token)
802	                )
803	                all_expand_queries.append(q_sub)
804	
805	    applicable = sum(1 for c in coverages if c.applies)
806	    covered_n = sum(1 for c in coverages if c.applies and c.covered)
807	    uncovered_n = applicable - covered_n
808	
809	    notes: list[str] = []
810	    if uncovered_n > 0:
811	        uncovered_labels = [
812	            c.topic.label for c in coverages
813	            if c.applies and not c.covered
814	        ]
815	        notes.append(
816	            f"{uncovered_n}/{applicable} applicable topic(s) uncovered: "
817	            f"{uncovered_labels}"
818	        )
819	    # BUG-7 (I-arch-006, #1262): surface any critical-topic applicability disclosure
820	    # — a fail-closed (recognizer-unavailable) note OR a pure-disclose (recognizer
821	    # confident-negative) note — so the decision is DISCLOSED, never silent.
822	    notes.extend(applicability_disclosures)
823	
824	    return CompletenessReport(
825	        domain=resolved_domain,
826	        topics=coverages,
827	        total_applicable=applicable,
828	        total_covered=covered_n,
829	        total_uncovered=uncovered_n,
830	        expand_queries=all_expand_queries,
831	        notes=notes,
832	    )
833

1	"""I-ready-006 (#1082): deterministic query-complexity router (no model, offline, §8.4-safe).
2	
3	Classifies a research question as ``simple`` (a right-sizeable factual lookup), ``complex`` (the full
4	heavyweight deep-research path), or ``moderate``. Pure stdlib + ``re`` — no LLM call (burning an LLM
5	call just to decide a query is simple would defeat the cost-saving point) and no model load.
6	
7	FAIL-OPEN by design (clinical-safety): anything NOT confidently ``simple`` is classified ``complex``,
8	so a hard clinical / policy / comparison / mechanism question is NEVER under-served. The caller wires
9	this behind ``PG_COMPLEXITY_ROUTING`` (default OFF) and only right-sizes when
10	``complexity == "simple"`` AND ``confidence >= PG_COMPLEXITY_MIN_CONFIDENCE``.
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import re
15	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
16	
17	
18	@dataclass
19	class ComplexityDecision:
20	    """The routing decision. ``reasons`` is auditable telemetry for the manifest."""
21	
22	    complexity: str                       # "simple" | "moderate" | "complex"
23	    confidence: float                     # 0.0 - 1.0
24	    reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
25	
26	
27	# Intent markers that force the FULL heavyweight path — comparison / causal / mechanism / synthesis /
28	# clinical-evaluative. Presence of ANY ⇒ NOT simple (fail-open to complex). A clinical dosing / safety
29	# / efficacy / mechanism question is deliberately routed complex: better to OVER-serve it than under-
30	# serve it. NOTE: a bare conjunction ("Telus AND Bell ...") is a parallel FACTUAL lookup, not a
31	# comparison — only explicit compare/versus/difference verbs count here.
32	_COMPLEX_INTENT = re.compile(
33	    r"\b(compare|comparison|versus|vs\.?|difference between|trade[- ]?off|"
34	    r"why|how does|how do|mechanism|pathophysiolog|causal|cause[sd]?|effect of|impact of|"
35	    r"efficacy|safety|adverse|contraindicat|dose|dosing|titrat|regimen|"
36	    r"meta[- ]?analysis|systematic review|guideline|recommend|"
37	    r"relationship between|associat|correlat|predict|forecast|project|"
38	    r"should|optimal|best (?:approach|strategy|treatment|option)|evaluate|assess(?:ment)?|"
39	    r"pros and cons|advantages|risk[- ]?benefit|"
40	    # I-ready-006 (#1082) Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1-2: analytical / due-diligence intent — these are
41	    # NOT one-line factual lookups (e.g. "Apple revenue drivers and competitive risks", "Microsoft
42	    # revenue exposure to OpenAI", "Apple profit risk from China tariffs"). Any of these ⇒ full path.
43	    r"driver|risk|exposure|competiti|outlook|threat|headwind|tailwind|moat|"
44	    r"opportunit|strateg|tariff|sanction|next \d+ years|over the next|going forward|"
45	    r"implication|scenario|sensitivity|downside|upside|catalyst|"
46	    # iter-4 P1-2: investment-JUDGMENT markers — "Is Tesla stock overvalued/a buy?" is an analysis,
47	    # not a factual lookup. Structural (no ticker enumeration).
48	    r"overvalued|undervalued|fair[- ]?value|fairly valued|\bbuy\b|\bsell\b|\bhold\b|"
49	    r"worth buying|good investment|invest in|bullish|bearish|price target|"
50	    r"should i (?:buy|sell|invest)|is it a (?:buy|sell))\b",
51	    re.IGNORECASE,
52	)
53	
54	# I-ready-006 (#1082) Codex diff-gate iter-4 P1-1: COHORT-PREVALENCE / drug-utilization pattern —
55	# "population/number/prevalence/proportion of <cohort> WITH/TAKING/USING/ON/DIAGNOSED <X>" is an
56	# epidemiology query, NOT a civic "population of <place>" fact. STRUCTURAL (catches the whole class
57	# without enumerating disease names, per Codex's blocker). Any hit ⇒ full path.
58	_COHORT_PREVALENCE = re.compile(
59	    r"\b(?:population|number|prevalence|proportion|percentage|share|count|fraction)\s+of\b"
60	    r".{0,60}?\b(?:with|who\s+(?:have|has|are|use|take)|taking|using|on\s+|diagnosed|"
61	    r"suffering|affected|living with|prescribed|treated)\b",
62	    re.IGNORECASE,
63	)
64	
65	# Clinical / medical / health / epidemiology content. ANY hit ⇒ NOT simple (fail-open to complex),
66	# regardless of a factual cue — a clinical safety/outcome question ("mortality rate of Semaglutide",
67	# "incidence of Guillain-Barré after Shingrix") must NEVER be right-sized to a 1-source answer (Codex
68	# diff-gate iter-1 P1-1; lethal-class under-serving per CLAUDE.md §-1.1). Erring toward complex here
69	# is the SAFE direction (over-serving a stock query that trips a drug-suffix is harmless; under-
70	# serving a clinical query is not).
71	# Word/STEM group — matched as PREFIXES via the trailing \w* (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1-1: the prior
72	# \b-bounded stems did NOT match "obesity"/"diabetes"/"statins"). \b(?:stem)\w* matches the stem plus
73	# any continuation. Includes common disease/condition names + abbreviations the allowlist flip alone
74	# might not veto.
75	_CLINICAL_CONTENT = re.compile(
76	    r"\b(?:patient|disease|syndrome|disorder|infect|cancer|tumou?r|diabet|obes|hypertens|"
77	    r"cardiovascular|cardiac|renal|hepatic|pulmonary|respiratory|neuro|psychiatr|oncolog|"
78	    r"mortality|morbidity|incidence|prevalence|survival|prognos|remission|relaps|recurren|"
79	    r"complication|hospitali|readmission|cure rate|case fatality|fatality|fatal|death|deaths|"
80	    r"epidemi|pandemic|outbreak|covid|coronavirus|influenza|ebola|measles|sepsis|stroke|"
81	    r"guillain|gbs|long covid|"
82	    r"drug|medication|therap|treatment|vaccin|immuni|dose|dosing|dosage|clinical|diagnos|symptom|"
83	    r"adverse|side[- ]?effect|toxicit|contraindicat|comorbid|"
84	    r"semaglutide|tirzepatide|ozempic|wegovy|mounjaro|metformin|warfarin|statin)\w*"
85	    r"|\b\w+(?:mab|nib|tinib|gliptin|glutide|afil|statin|sartan|cycline|cillin|mycin|"
86	    r"parin|setron|grel|zepine)\b",
87	    re.IGNORECASE,
88	)
89	
90	# ALLOWLIST of clearly NON-medical factual domains that qualify a query as right-sizeable "simple".
91	# This is OPT-IN (a query is simple ONLY if it matches a known-safe factual cue) rather than OPT-OUT of
92	# a clinical DENYLIST — a denylist can never be complete (Codex diff-gate iter-2: the clinical guard
93	# missed "death" / "fatality" / "GBS" / "COVID" / "Shingrix"). With the allowlist, "death rate from
94	# COVID-19" / "rate of GBS after Shingrix" have NO safe factual cue ⇒ fail open to complex regardless
95	# of whether the denylist names that disease. Financial / economic / corporate / geographic / civic
96	# facts only — NOT generic "rate of" / "how many", which can be a clinical outcome rate.
97	_SAFE_FACTUAL_CUE = re.compile(
98	    r"\b(stock price|share price|stock|shares|market cap|market capitali[sz]ation|"
99	    r"revenue|profit|earnings|dividend|valuation|net worth|ipo|"
100	    r"gdp|inflation rate|unemployment rate|exchange rate|interest rate|"
101	    # I-ready-006 (#1082) Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1-1: "population OF <place>" is a civic fact, but bare
102	    # "population" allowed epidemiology ("population WITH obesity", "population TAKING statins") to route
103	    # simple. Require "population of"; drug-utilization / disease-prevalence phrasings lose the safe cue
104	    # (and the clinical guard above is the second line of defense).
105	    r"population of|capital of|area of|time ?zone|headquarter|founded|founder|"
106	    r"\bceo\b|number of employees|ticker|stock exchange)\b",
107	    re.IGNORECASE,
108	)
109	
110	_TEMPORAL_RANGE = re.compile(
111	    r"\b(over (?:the )?(?:past|last)\s+\d+\s+years?|in \d{4}|since \d{4}|\d+[- ]year)\b",
112	    re.IGNORECASE,
113	)
114	
115	
116	def classify_complexity(question: str) -> ComplexityDecision:
117	    """Classify ``question``. Deterministic, fail-open. Never raises — a malformed input returns a
118	    low-confidence ``complex`` decision so the caller takes the full path."""
119	    try:
120	        q = (question or "").strip()
121	        if not q:
122	            return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.0, ["empty_question_fail_open"])
123	
124	        reasons: list[str] = []
125	        # Clinical/medical content + the cohort-prevalence pattern force the FULL path FIRST (highest
126	        # priority) — a clinical safety/outcome/epidemiology question is never right-sized (Codex
127	        # diff-gate iter-1/4 P1-1).
128	        if _CLINICAL_CONTENT.search(q):
129	            return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.9, ["clinical_medical_content"])
130	        if _COHORT_PREVALENCE.search(q):
131	            return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.9, ["cohort_prevalence_pattern"])
132	
133	        # Any explicit compare/causal/mechanism/synthesis/investment-judgment intent ⇒ full path.
134	        if _COMPLEX_INTENT.search(q):
135	            return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.9, ["complex_intent_marker"])
136	
137	        has_safe_factual = bool(_SAFE_FACTUAL_CUE.search(q))
138	        has_temporal = bool(_TEMPORAL_RANGE.search(q))
139	
140	        # Named-entity proxy: a capitalized token NOT at sentence start (so the leading "What"/"How"
141	        # is excluded), OR an all-caps ticker (2-5 letters). Strip trailing punctuation so "France?" /
142	        # "Canada?" still count as entities (Codex diff-gate iter-4 P2 — else they only reach 0.70 and
143	        # miss the 0.80 right-sizing gate; this over-serves, but it's the safe direction).
144	        words = q.split()
145	        caps = [
146	            w.rstrip("?.!,;:") for w in words[1:]
147	            if re.match(r"^[A-Z][A-Za-z.&-]+[?.!,;:]?$", w)
148	        ]
149	        has_entity = len(caps) >= 1 or bool(re.search(r"\b[A-Z]{2,5}\b", q))
150	        word_count = len(words)
151	
152	        # SIMPLE is OPT-IN: it REQUIRES a SAFE non-medical factual cue (financial/economic/civic). A
153	        # query with no safe cue — incl. any clinical outcome/safety rate the denylist might miss —
154	        # FAILS OPEN to complex. Multi-entity is fine (e.g. "Telus and Bell stock price over 20 years").
155	        if has_safe_factual and has_entity and word_count <= 25:
156	            reasons.append("safe_factual_cue+named_entity")
157	            if has_temporal:
158	                reasons.append("temporal_range")
159	            return ComplexityDecision("simple", 0.85, reasons)
160	
161	        # A short safe-factual question without a clear named entity is a weaker simple signal.
162	        if has_safe_factual and word_count <= 15:
163	            reasons.append("safe_factual_cue_short")
164	            return ComplexityDecision("simple", 0.70, reasons)
165	
166	        # No SAFE factual cue ⇒ FAIL OPEN to the full heavyweight path.
167	        reasons.append("no_safe_factual_signal_fail_open")
168	        return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.50, reasons)
169	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never let the router abort a run; fail open to complex.
170	        return ComplexityDecision("complex", 0.0, [f"router_error_fail_open:{type(exc).__name__}"])
171

1	"""M-57 (2026-04-23): V30 contract-driven outline composer.
2	
3	V30 Report Contract Architecture Layer 3.
4	
5	Replaces LLM-emergent outline planning with CONTRACT-DETERMINED
6	outline structure. Given a CompiledFrame (M-55) and the retrieved
7	FrameRows (M-56), emit a ContractOutline where:
8	
9	  - Sections come from `contract.section_order` (explicit; no
10	    alphabetic-by-label fragility — Codex M-55 audit Medium fix).
11	  - Subsections come from `contract.rendering_slots`, sorted by
12	    `slot.ordering` within each section.
13	  - Each slot's `entity_ids` come from `contract.entities_by_slot()`
14	    in compiler-deterministic order.
15	  - Each slot carries its frame rows' provenance_classes so M-58
16	    prompt assembly + M-60 manifest rendering know at a glance
17	    whether the slot is gap vs OA vs abstract-only.
18	
19	## Design principles
20	
21	1. **Pure function.** No LLM, no network, no wall-clock. Same
22	   inputs → byte-identical ContractOutline.
23	2. **Gap-slot preservation.** Slots whose frame rows ALL classify
24	   as FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE still appear in the outline with
25	   `is_gap=True`. M-60 renders explicit gap content; silent
26	   omission is forbidden (Codex plan review #4).
27	3. **Entity-type-agnostic.** No per-type branching. Works for
28	   pivotal_trial + mechanism_primary + regulatory today, and
29	   for statute / dft_primary / whatever else later without code
30	   changes (Codex review #7, M-62 generalization proof).
31	4. **Stable identifiers through the pipeline.** `ContractSlotPlan.entity_ids`
32	   holds the contract-side entity ids (e.g. `surpass_2_primary`).
33	   M-58/M-59/M-60 consume these; no reliance on opaque ev_xxx
34	   rewriting.
35	5. **Intra-slot ordering inherited, not re-asserted.** Within-slot
36	   entity order is READ from `CompiledFrame.ordered_entity_ids`
37	   (M-55), not re-sorted by M-57. If the compiler ordering policy
38	   evolves (e.g. per-entity priority key), M-57 picks up the new
39	   order for free. Codex M-57 audit Medium fix.
40	
41	## What M-57 does NOT do
42	
43	- Does NOT call the LLM outline planner. Enrichment sections
44	  (Contradictions / Limitations) stay with the existing
45	  LLM planner — M-57 covers only contract-slot sections.
46	- Does NOT touch `multi_section_generator.py`. That integration
47	  is M-58's responsibility (slot-bound prompts consume this
48	  outline). Keeping M-57 standalone avoids churn in the 2500-line
49	  generator module.
50	- Does NOT fetch content. M-56 did that. M-57 only arranges.
51	
52	## Relationship to existing `SectionPlan`
53	
54	`ContractOutline.to_section_plan_dicts()` produces a list of
55	`{"title", "focus", "ev_ids"}` dicts — the legacy SectionPlan
56	payload shape — so existing generator code can consume the
57	flattened view while M-58 consumes the structured view. The
58	actual `SectionPlan` dataclass lives in
59	`generator/multi_section_generator.py`; M-57 stays free of
60	generator imports.

71	@dataclass(frozen=True)
72	class ContractSlotPlan:
73	    """One rendering slot's plan within a section.
74	
75	    Represents a subsection in the rendered report: header from
76	    `subsection_title`, content from `entity_ids` resolved against
77	    FrameRows.
78	    """
79	
80	    slot_id: str
81	    section: str
82	    subsection_title: str
83	    ordering: int
84	    entity_ids: tuple[str, ...]
85	    # M-56 frame row snapshot per entity, parallel to entity_ids.
86	    provenance_classes: tuple[str, ...]
87	    # True iff every frame row for this slot is gap-unrecoverable.
88	    # M-60 renders explicit gap content for is_gap=True slots.
89	    is_gap: bool
90	    # Did M-56 retrieve ANY resolvable content? Used for "partial"
91	    # reporting when a multi-entity slot has one gap + one fill.
92	    is_partial: bool

95	@dataclass(frozen=True)
96	class ContractSectionPlan:
97	    """One section, composed of rendering slots in slot.ordering
98	    order. `focus` is a one-line deterministic summary for the
99	    section-level prompt header (used as-is in the legacy
100	    SectionPlan adapter; M-58 uses the structured slots instead).
101	    """
102	
103	    section: str
104	    section_ordering_index: int
105	    slots: tuple[ContractSlotPlan, ...]
106	    focus: str

109	@dataclass(frozen=True)
110	class ContractOutline:
111	    """Full contract-driven outline. M-57 output."""
112	
113	    research_question: str
114	    schema_version: str
115	    sections: tuple[ContractSectionPlan, ...]
116	
117	    def slots_by_id(self) -> dict[str, ContractSlotPlan]:
118	        out: dict[str, ContractSlotPlan] = {}
119	        for sec in self.sections:
120	            for slot in sec.slots:
121	                out[slot.slot_id] = slot
122	        return out
123	
124	    def sections_by_name(self) -> dict[str, ContractSectionPlan]:
125	        return {s.section: s for s in self.sections}
126	
127	    def all_entity_ids(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
128	        """Flattened entity ids across every slot across every
129	        section, in contract rendering order."""
130	        out: list[str] = []
131	        for sec in self.sections:
132	            for slot in sec.slots:
133	                out.extend(slot.entity_ids)
134	        return tuple(out)
135	
136	    def gap_slot_ids(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
137	        """Slot ids where all frame rows are gap-unrecoverable.
138	        M-60 manifest consumption."""
139	        return tuple(
140	            slot.slot_id
141	            for sec in self.sections
142	            for slot in sec.slots
143	            if slot.is_gap
144	        )
145	
146	    def to_section_plan_dicts(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
147	        """Legacy-compatible flattened view: one dict per section
148	        in `{"title", "focus", "ev_ids"}` shape. ev_ids are the
149	        contract entity ids (e.g. `surpass_2_primary`); the
150	        existing generator pipeline adapts those to its own
151	        evidence-pool ids as needed."""
152	        return [
153	            {
154	                "title": sec.section,
155	                "focus": sec.focus,
156	                "ev_ids": [
157	                    eid
158	                    for slot in sec.slots
159	                    for eid in slot.entity_ids
160	                ],
161	            }
162	            for sec in self.sections
163	        ]

166	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
167	# Compose
168	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	def compose_outline_from_contract(
170	    compiled_frame: CompiledFrame,
171	    frame_rows: tuple[FrameRow, ...],
172	) -> ContractOutline:
173	    """M-57 public entrypoint.
174	
175	    Args:
176	        compiled_frame: output of M-55 compile_frame(...).
177	        frame_rows: output of M-56 fetch_compiled_frame(...). Must
178	            be a tuple parallel to `compiled_frame.evidence_bindings`
179	            (same length, same order) — M-56's documented contract.
180	
181	    Returns:
182	        ContractOutline with sections in contract.section_order
183	        (or alphabetic when section_order is None) and slots in
184	        slot.ordering within each section.
185	
186	    Raises:
187	        ValueError: when `frame_rows` is not parallel to
188	            `compiled_frame.evidence_bindings`, or a frame row's
189	            entity_id doesn't match the binding at the same index.
190	    """
191	    _validate_frame_rows_parallel(compiled_frame, frame_rows)
192	
193	    # Build a fast entity_id → FrameRow lookup.
194	    rows_by_eid: dict[str, FrameRow] = {
195	        r.entity_id: r for r in frame_rows
196	    }
197	
198	    contract = compiled_frame.contract
199	    section_order = _resolve_section_order(contract)
200	
201	    # Codex M-57 audit Medium fix: within-slot entity ordering is
202	    # INHERITED from M-55 compiler, not re-sorted by M-57. The
203	    # compiler's ordered_entity_ids tuple is the single source of
204	    # truth for entity rendering order — M-57 only projects that
205	    # order onto slots.
206	    compiler_entity_rank = {
207	        eid: i for i, eid in enumerate(compiled_frame.ordered_entity_ids)
208	    }
209	
210	    # Group slots by section
211	    slots_by_section: dict[str, list[Any]] = {}
212	    for slot in contract.rendering_slots:
213	        slots_by_section.setdefault(slot.section, []).append(slot)
214	
215	    # Compose per-section
216	    section_plans: list[ContractSectionPlan] = []
217	    entities_by_slot = contract.entities_by_slot()
218	    for sec_idx, section_name in enumerate(section_order):
219	        raw_slots = slots_by_section.get(section_name, [])
220	        # Sort slots within section by ordering (then id for tiebreak)
221	        sorted_slots = sorted(raw_slots, key=lambda s: (s.ordering, s.id))
222	
223	        slot_plans: list[ContractSlotPlan] = []
224	        for slot in sorted_slots:
225	            entities = entities_by_slot.get(slot.id, [])
226	            # Inherit compiler-determined entity order. An entity
227	            # not present in the compiler ordering (defensive;
228	            # should not happen on a well-formed CompiledFrame)
229	            # sorts to the end by id.
230	            max_rank = len(compiler_entity_rank)
231	            entities_ordered = sorted(
232	                entities,
233	                key=lambda e: (
234	                    compiler_entity_rank.get(e.id, max_rank),
235	                    e.id,
236	                ),
237	            )
238	            eids = tuple(e.id for e in entities_ordered)
239	
240	            provenance_classes: list[str] = []
241	            gap_count = 0
242	            for eid in eids:
243	                row = rows_by_eid.get(eid)
244	                if row is None:
245	                    # Defensive: M-56 should have produced a row
246	                    # for every binding. Treat as gap.
247	                    provenance_classes.append(
248	                        ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE.value
249	                    )
250	                    gap_count += 1
251	                    continue
252	                provenance_classes.append(row.provenance_class.value)
253	                if row.is_gap():
254	                    gap_count += 1
255	
256	            is_gap = len(eids) > 0 and gap_count == len(eids)
257	            is_partial = 0 < gap_count < len(eids)
258	
259	            slot_plans.append(ContractSlotPlan(
260	                slot_id=slot.id,
261	                section=section_name,
262	                subsection_title=slot.subsection_title,
263	                ordering=slot.ordering,
264	                entity_ids=eids,
265	                provenance_classes=tuple(provenance_classes),
266	                is_gap=is_gap,
267	                is_partial=is_partial,
268	            ))
269	
270	        focus = _compose_section_focus(section_name, slot_plans)
271	        section_plans.append(ContractSectionPlan(
272	            section=section_name,
273	            section_ordering_index=sec_idx,
274	            slots=tuple(slot_plans),
275	            focus=focus,
276	        ))
277	
278	    return ContractOutline(
279	        research_question=compiled_frame.research_question,
280	        schema_version=compiled_frame.schema_version,
281	        sections=tuple(section_plans),
282	    )
283	
284	
285	def _validate_frame_rows_parallel(
286	    compiled_frame: CompiledFrame,
287	    frame_rows: tuple[FrameRow, ...],
288	) -> None:
289	    bindings = compiled_frame.evidence_bindings
290	    if len(frame_rows) != len(bindings):
291	        raise ValueError(
292	            f"frame_rows length {len(frame_rows)} does not match "
293	            f"evidence_bindings length {len(bindings)}; M-56 "
294	            f"fetch_compiled_frame must return rows parallel to "
295	            f"bindings"
296	        )
297	    for i, (b, r) in enumerate(zip(bindings, frame_rows)):
298	        if b.entity_id != r.entity_id:
299	            raise ValueError(
300	                f"frame_rows[{i}].entity_id={r.entity_id!r} does "
301	                f"not match bindings[{i}].entity_id={b.entity_id!r}; "
302	                f"M-56 ordering contract violated"
303	            )
304	
305	
306	def _resolve_section_order(contract: Any) -> tuple[str, ...]:
307	    """When contract declares section_order, honor it. Otherwise
308	    fall back to alphabetic-by-label.
309	
310	    NOTE: This policy is DUPLICATED in M-55 `_ordered_entities()`
311	    (src/polaris_graph/nodes/frame_compiler.py). Keep them in sync
312	    if the contract evolves (e.g. add a per-section priority key).
313	    A cross-layer integration test covers both behaviors on the
314	    real clinical.yaml, so drift shows up as test breakage.
315	
316	    The fallback is quiet here; M-55 already emitted the
317	    alphabetic-fallback warning at compile time, so M-57 would be
318	    redundant to re-emit it.
319	    """
320	    if contract.section_order is not None:
321	        return contract.section_order
322	    sections = sorted({s.section for s in contract.rendering_slots})
323	    return tuple(sections)
324	
325	
326	def _compose_section_focus(
327	    section_name: str, slot_plans: list[ContractSlotPlan],
328	) -> str:
329	    """Deterministic one-line focus for section-level prompt. Lists
330	    subsection titles and flags gaps.
331	
332	    Format examples:
333	      - "3 subsections: SURPASS-1, SURPASS-2, SURPASS-3"
334	      - "2 subsections with 1 gap: Thomas clamp, mechanism TBD"
335	
336	    Entity-type-agnostic: no special-casing per section name. Works
337	    for novel sections in a non-clinical slug."""
338	    n = len(slot_plans)
339	    if n == 0:
340	        return f"{section_name}: no contract-bound subsections"
341	
342	    gap_count = sum(1 for s in slot_plans if s.is_gap)
343	    partial_count = sum(1 for s in slot_plans if s.is_partial)
344	    titles = [s.subsection_title for s in slot_plans]
345	
346	    # Cap title list for one-line readability
347	    title_list = ", ".join(titles[:6])
348	    if len(titles) > 6:
349	        title_list = f"{title_list}, ... (+{len(titles) - 6} more)"
350	
351	    suffix = ""
352	    if gap_count:
353	        suffix += f"; {gap_count} gap"
354	        if gap_count > 1:
355	            suffix += "s"
356	    if partial_count:
357	        suffix += f"; {partial_count} partial"
358	
359	    return (
360	        f"{n} subsection{'s' if n > 1 else ''}: {title_list}{suffix}"
361	    )

1	"""
2	Corpus-adequacy gate — HONEST-REBUILD R-6 Gap-1.
3	
4	Decides whether the retrieved+classified corpus is strong enough for
5	synthesis. Prevents the silent failure mode where the pipeline ships a
6	150-word report with a "13/13 rule checks pass" badge even though the
7	corpus had 3 T1 sources and a 400-word report is misleadingly brief.
8	
9	DECISION SPACE:
10	    PROCEED: corpus meets domain thresholds; synthesize normally
11	    EXPAND:  corpus short of thresholds but close; run a second
12	             retrieval round with broader queries (caller decides)
13	    ABORT:   corpus is too thin for confident synthesis; caller should
14	             either abort, ask the user to approve a short report, or
15	             widen retrieval significantly
16	
17	DOES NOT MAKE NETWORK CALLS. Pure function — tier counts + thresholds.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import logging
22	import os
23	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
24	from typing import Any, Literal
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.corpus_adequacy_gate")
27	
28	
29	AdequacyDecision = Literal["proceed", "expand", "abort"]
30	
31	
32	# ── BUG-20 (I-arch-011): exclude content-less stubs from the grounded count ────
33	# THE BUG: the adequacy gate counted EVERY retrieved row toward `evidence_rows`
34	# (the grounded-content threshold), including rows the fetch/tier layer flagged as
35	# content-less stubs — a fetch that failed, a landing page with no body, or a
36	# content-starved span. Counting 91 such stubs as valid sources produced a
37	# FALSE adequacy PASS (decision=proceed) on a corpus with no real grounded content.
38	#
39	# THE FIX: when the caller passes the actual `evidence_rows`, count toward the
40	# grounded `evidence_rows` finding ONLY rows that are NOT degraded. A row is
41	# degraded iff any of these flags is truthy on the row dict. `fetch_degraded` is
42	# the semantic boolean the tier/authority layer sets; the concrete per-row flags
43	# the fetch layer already writes (live_retriever.py) are read as the union so the
44	# fix fires on the REAL production rows, not only on an aspirational future flag.
45	#
46	# We exclude ONLY content-less stubs (the flags below). A merely down-weighted but
47	# GROUNDED row (`down_weighted` / low `relevance_weight`) is NOT excluded — per the
48	# WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (§-1.3) a low-weight grounded source is still a real
49	# source; dropping it would be a banned breadth cap. This change only STOPS a
50	# false PASS; it never relaxes a real gate and adds no cap/floor.
51	_DEGRADED_ROW_FLAGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
52	    "fetch_degraded",
53	    "content_starved",
54	    "fetch_failed",
55	    "landing_page",
56	)
57	
58	
59	def _row_is_content_less_stub(row: Any) -> bool:
60	    """True iff ``row`` is a content-less stub per any degraded flag.
61	
62	    Reads the union of the tier/authority `fetch_degraded` boolean and the
63	    concrete fetch-layer flags (`content_starved` / `fetch_failed` /
64	    `landing_page`). A non-dict row is treated as not-a-stub (defensive — the
65	    caller filters rows elsewhere); only truthy flags exclude a row.
66	    """
67	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
68	        return False
69	    return any(bool(row.get(flag)) for flag in _DEGRADED_ROW_FLAGS)
70	
71	
72	def count_grounded_rows(evidence_rows: list[Any]) -> int:
73	    """Count evidence rows that carry real grounded content (BUG-20).
74	
75	    Excludes content-less stubs (see ``_row_is_content_less_stub``). Used by
76	    :func:`assess_corpus_adequacy` for the `evidence_rows` grounded threshold so
77	    a stub-padded corpus cannot false-PASS adequacy.
78	    """
79	    return sum(1 for r in evidence_rows if not _row_is_content_less_stub(r))
80	
81	
82	# ── I-deepfix-001 B7 (#1351): on-topic adequacy predicate ──────────────────────
83	# THE BUG (§-1.1 BANNED pattern-presence signal): assess_corpus_adequacy was a
84	# PURE tier-count gate with ZERO relevance dimension — fresh2 reported
85	# decision=proceed over a pool the 9-lens forensic flagged ~12/27 off-topic
86	# (incl. an Anubis bot-wall tiered T1). A contaminated pool false-PASSED.
87	#
88	# THE FIX (gate-honesty only; faithfulness engine UNTOUCHED — strict_verify /
89	# NLI / 4-role / D8 / provenance never read this): when the caller passes the
90	# ACTUAL evidence_rows, a row counts toward the grounded / tier denominators ONLY
91	# if it is on-topic — its topical-relevance WEIGHT (the per-row `relevance_weight`
92	# the B4 retrieval-relevance scorer writes at live_retriever.py ~L5108) is at or
93	# above a DISCLOSED floor (PG_ADEQUACY_RELEVANCE_FLOOR, default 0.30, §-1.3
94	# disclosed weight-floor — never a silent source DROP; the row still flows to
95	# composition and the faithfulness engine).
96	#
97	# FAIL-OPEN (the wave-1 P0 class): a row with NO explicit `relevance_weight` key
98	# (every OFF-path / legacy / seed row, and any pre-B4 row) is treated as ON-TOPIC.
99	# Only a row carrying an EXPLICIT weight BELOW the floor is demoted from the
100	# denominator. This preserves "byte-identical when the B4 weight is absent" and
101	# can never collapse a legacy run's grounded count to a false ABORT.
102	_ENV_RELEVANCE_FLOOR = "PG_ADEQUACY_RELEVANCE_FLOOR"
103	_DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR = 0.30
104	
105	
106	def _adequacy_relevance_floor() -> float:
107	    """Disclosed on-topic weight floor (LAW VI). Clamp to [0, 1]; a misconfigured
108	    value outside that range falls back to the default."""
109	    raw = os.getenv(_ENV_RELEVANCE_FLOOR, "").strip()
110	    if not raw:
111	        return _DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR
112	    try:
113	        val = float(raw)
114	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
115	        return _DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR
116	    return min(max(val, 0.0), 1.0)
117	
118	
119	def _row_is_on_topic(row: Any, floor: float) -> bool:
120	    """True iff ``row`` is on-topic for the adequacy denominators (B7).
121	
122	    FAIL-OPEN: a non-dict row, or a row with NO explicit ``relevance_weight``
123	    key, counts as ON-TOPIC (missing weight is never treated as off-topic — that
124	    would nuke every legacy/OFF-path run). Only an explicit numeric weight BELOW
125	    ``floor`` makes a row off-topic. A non-numeric weight also fails open.
126	    """
127	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
128	        return True
129	    if "relevance_weight" not in row:
130	        return True
131	    try:
132	        w = float(row.get("relevance_weight"))
133	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
134	        return True
135	    return w >= floor
136	
137	
138	def count_on_topic_grounded_rows(evidence_rows: list[Any], floor: float) -> int:
139	    """Grounded rows (BUG-20 stub filter) that are ALSO on-topic (B7).
140	
141	    A row counts iff it is NOT a content-less stub AND it is on-topic at ``floor``.
142	    """
143	    return sum(
144	        1 for r in evidence_rows
145	        if not _row_is_content_less_stub(r) and _row_is_on_topic(r, floor)
146	    )
147	
148	
149	def _on_topic_tier_counts(
150	    evidence_rows: list[Any], floor: float, raw_tier_counts: dict[str, int],
151	) -> dict[str, int]:
152	    """Re-tally tier_counts over ON-TOPIC rows only (B7).
153	
154	    Each evidence row's tier is read from ``row['tier']`` (the surface the
155	    classifier back-fills). A row with no usable tier is bucketed UNKNOWN so the
156	    total still reflects every on-topic row. FAIL-OPEN: if NO row carries a tier
157	    key at all, the on-topic re-tally is empty and the caller falls back to the
158	    raw classifier ``tier_counts`` (so an evidence_rows pool that simply lacks the
159	    tier key never zeroes the gate).
160	    """
161	    counts: dict[str, int] = {}
162	    saw_tier = False
163	    for r in evidence_rows:
164	        if not isinstance(r, dict):
165	            continue
166	        if _row_is_content_less_stub(r) or not _row_is_on_topic(r, floor):
167	            continue
168	        tier = r.get("tier")
169	        if tier:
170	            saw_tier = True
171	            key = str(tier)
172	        else:
173	            key = "UNKNOWN"
174	        counts[key] = counts.get(key, 0) + 1
175	    # FAIL-OPEN: no per-row tier signal at all → keep the classifier tier_counts.
176	    if not saw_tier:
177	        return dict(raw_tier_counts)
178	    return counts
179

283	        # (StatsCan, OECD, McKinsey); gov-stats agencies surface as T4

296	def _get_thresholds(
297	    domain: str,
298	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
299	    override: AdequacyThresholds | None = None,
300	) -> AdequacyThresholds:
301	    """Resolve adequacy thresholds with override > protocol > default."""
302	    if override is not None:
303	        return override
304	    # Protocol may carry a corpus_adequacy block
305	    if protocol:
306	        ca = protocol.get("corpus_adequacy") or {}
307	        if ca:
308	            base = _DEFAULT_DOMAIN_THRESHOLDS.get(domain, AdequacyThresholds())
309	            # Merge: take protocol values where present
310	            return AdequacyThresholds(
311	                min_total_sources=int(ca.get("min_total_sources", base.min_total_sources)),
312	                min_t1_count=int(ca.get("min_t1_count", base.min_t1_count)),
313	                min_t1_plus_t2=int(ca.get("min_t1_plus_t2", base.min_t1_plus_t2)),
314	                min_t1_plus_t2_plus_t3=int(ca.get("min_t1_plus_t2_plus_t3", base.min_t1_plus_t2_plus_t3)),
315	                min_t3_plus_t4_plus_t6=int(ca.get("min_t3_plus_t4_plus_t6", base.min_t3_plus_t4_plus_t6)),
316	                min_evidence_rows=int(ca.get("min_evidence_rows", base.min_evidence_rows)),
317	                max_t5_plus_t6_fraction=float(ca.get("max_t5_plus_t6_fraction", base.max_t5_plus_t6_fraction)),
318	                max_t7_fraction=float(ca.get("max_t7_fraction", base.max_t7_fraction)),
319	                abort_if_below_fraction=float(ca.get("abort_if_below_fraction", base.abort_if_below_fraction)),
320	            )
321	    return _DEFAULT_DOMAIN_THRESHOLDS.get(domain, AdequacyThresholds())
322

324	def assess_corpus_adequacy(
325	    *,
326	    tier_counts: dict[str, int],
327	    evidence_row_count: int,
328	    domain: str,
329	    protocol: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
330	    override: AdequacyThresholds | None = None,
331	    evidence_rows: list[Any] | None = None,
332	) -> CorpusAdequacyReport:
333	    """Return an AdequacyReport for the retrieved corpus.
334	
335	    Args:
336	        tier_counts: dict mapping "T1"/"T2"/.../"T7"/"UNKNOWN" -> count.
337	        evidence_row_count: number of evidence rows AFTER content-starved
338	            filtering (Fix-D). Used for the grounded `evidence_rows` threshold
339	            ONLY when ``evidence_rows`` is not supplied (back-compat path).
340	        domain: clinical / policy / tech / due_diligence.
341	        protocol: dict form of protocol.json (may carry adequacy overrides).
342	        override: explicit AdequacyThresholds, wins over all else.
343	        evidence_rows: BUG-20 (I-arch-011) — the ACTUAL evidence row dicts. When
344	            supplied, the grounded `evidence_rows` finding counts only rows that
345	            are NOT content-less stubs (excludes `fetch_degraded` /
346	            `content_starved` / `fetch_failed` / `landing_page` rows) so a
347	            stub-padded corpus cannot false-PASS adequacy. When None, behaviour is
348	            byte-identical to the prior callers (uses ``evidence_row_count``).
349	            WIRING NOTE: the run-script wiring pass should pass
350	            ``evidence_rows=retrieval.evidence_rows`` at each call site.
351	    """
352	    thr = _get_thresholds(domain, protocol, override)
353	
354	    # BUG-20: when the real rows are available, the grounded evidence count
355	    # EXCLUDES content-less stubs. Falls back to evidence_row_count otherwise.
356	    # I-deepfix-001 B7 (#1351): when real rows are available, the grounded count
357	    # ALSO excludes OFF-topic rows (explicit relevance_weight below the disclosed
358	    # floor) — gate denominators must reflect on-topic grounded content, not raw
359	    # tier presence (the §-1.1 BANNED pattern-presence signal). Fail-open: a row
360	    # without an explicit weight counts as on-topic (see ``_row_is_on_topic``).
361	    _floor = _adequacy_relevance_floor()
362	    raw_grounded = 0
363	    on_topic_grounded = 0
364	    # `tier_counts` is the CLASSIFIER tier histogram (the legacy gate input). When
365	    # real rows are supplied we re-tally tier_counts over ON-TOPIC rows so the
366	    # tier thresholds are computed over on-topic content too; otherwise the gate
367	    # is byte-identical to the prior callers.
368	    on_topic_tier_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
369	    if evidence_rows is not None:
370	        raw_grounded = count_grounded_rows(evidence_rows)
371	        on_topic_grounded = count_on_topic_grounded_rows(evidence_rows, _floor)
372	        grounded_evidence_rows = on_topic_grounded
373	        on_topic_tier_counts = _on_topic_tier_counts(
374	            evidence_rows, _floor, tier_counts,
375	        )
376	        gate_tier_counts = on_topic_tier_counts
377	    else:
378	        grounded_evidence_rows = evidence_row_count
379	        raw_grounded = evidence_row_count
380	        on_topic_grounded = evidence_row_count
381	        gate_tier_counts = dict(tier_counts)
382	
383	    total = sum(gate_tier_counts.values())
384	    t1 = gate_tier_counts.get("T1", 0)
385	    t2 = gate_tier_counts.get("T2", 0)
386	    t3 = gate_tier_counts.get("T3", 0)
387	    t4 = gate_tier_counts.get("T4", 0)
388	    t5 = gate_tier_counts.get("T5", 0)
389	    t6 = gate_tier_counts.get("T6", 0)
390	    t7 = gate_tier_counts.get("T7", 0)
391	
392	    findings: list[AdequacyFinding] = []
393	
394	    def _record(name: str, observed: float, threshold: float,
395	                direction: str, critical_at: float | None = None) -> None:
396	        if direction == "min":
397	            ok = observed >= threshold
398	            # below 50% of threshold = critical
399	            if critical_at is None:
400	                critical_at = threshold * thr.abort_if_below_fraction
401	            severity = ("ok" if ok else
402	                        "critical" if observed <= critical_at else
403	                        "warn")
404	        else:  # "max"
405	            ok = observed <= threshold
406	            # 1.5x over = critical
407	            if critical_at is None:
408	                critical_at = threshold * 1.5
409	            severity = ("ok" if ok else
410	                        "critical" if observed >= critical_at else
411	                        "warn")
412	        findings.append(AdequacyFinding(
413	            name=name, ok=ok, observed=observed,
414	            threshold=threshold, severity=severity,
415	        ))
416	
417	    _record("total_sources", total, thr.min_total_sources, "min")
418	    _record("t1_count", t1, thr.min_t1_count, "min")
419	    _record("t1_plus_t2", t1 + t2, thr.min_t1_plus_t2, "min")
420	    _record("t1_plus_t2_plus_t3", t1 + t2 + t3,
421	            thr.min_t1_plus_t2_plus_t3, "min")
422	    # GH#405: real quality signal for emerging-policy domains.
423	    _record("t3_plus_t4_plus_t6", t3 + t4 + t6,
424	            thr.min_t3_plus_t4_plus_t6, "min")
425	    # BUG-20: grounded count (stubs excluded when real rows were supplied).
426	    _record("evidence_rows", grounded_evidence_rows,
427	            thr.min_evidence_rows, "min")
428	
429	    low_quality_fraction = (t5 + t6) / max(total, 1)
430	    _record("low_quality_fraction",
431	            low_quality_fraction, thr.max_t5_plus_t6_fraction, "max")
432	
433	    t7_fraction = t7 / max(total, 1)
434	    _record("t7_fraction", t7_fraction, thr.max_t7_fraction, "max")
435	
436	    critical_count = sum(1 for f in findings if f.severity == "critical")
437	    warn_count = sum(1 for f in findings if f.severity == "warn")
438	
439	    if critical_count > 0:
440	        decision: AdequacyDecision = "abort"
441	    elif warn_count > 0:
442	        decision = "expand"
443	    else:
444	        decision = "proceed"
445	
446	    notes: list[str] = []
447	    if decision == "abort":
448	        failing = [f.name for f in findings if f.severity == "critical"]
449	        notes.append(
450	            f"Corpus fails {len(failing)} critical threshold(s): {failing}. "
451	            f"Refusing to synthesize a confident report; caller should "
452	            f"expand retrieval substantially or ABORT."
453	        )
454	    elif decision == "expand":
455	        warning = [f.name for f in findings if f.severity == "warn"]
456	        notes.append(
457	            f"Corpus below nominal on {len(warning)} threshold(s): {warning}. "
458	            f"Synthesis possible but caller is encouraged to trigger a "
459	            f"second retrieval round before proceeding."
460	        )
461	
462	    # I-deepfix-001 B7 (#1351): disclose the on-topic vs raw denominators so the
463	    # gate honesty is auditable (and the §-1.1 contaminated-pool false-PASS is
464	    # visible). Only when real rows were supplied AND off-topic rows were demoted.
465	    if evidence_rows is not None and on_topic_grounded < raw_grounded:
466	        notes.append(
467	            f"Adequacy computed over ON-TOPIC grounded rows "
468	            f"({on_topic_grounded}) — {raw_grounded - on_topic_grounded} "
469	            f"grounded row(s) demoted from the denominator as off-topic "
470	            f"(relevance_weight < {_floor:.2f}); raw grounded pool="
471	            f"{raw_grounded}. Demoted rows are KEPT in the corpus (§-1.3), "
472	            f"only excluded from the sufficiency count."
473	        )
474	
475	    # I-deepfix-001 W01-fx06 (#1344): the DECISION above (proceed/expand/abort) and
476	    # all on-topic disclosure ride on `gate_tier_counts` / `on_topic_*` — that is
477	    # UNCHANGED (methods-honesty preserved byte-for-byte). But the REPORTED
478	    # `total_sources` / `tier_counts` are restored to the CLASSIFIER population (the
479	    # passed-in `tier_counts` arg = corpus_approval `dist` counts), exactly as
480	    # pre-7000627a. RATIONALE: the FX-06 self-consistency tripwire in the spine
481	    # (run_honest_sweep_r3.py ~9341) compares `adequacy.total_sources` /
482	    # `adequacy.tier_counts` against the approval `dist` population. When B7 re-tallied
483	    # the REPORTED counts over ON-TOPIC evidence_rows, the two populations diverged BY
484	    # CONSTRUCTION whenever ANY row was demoted off-topic (e.g. 833 classified vs 639
485	    # on-topic), firing error_corpus_population_mismatch on EVERY normal run before a
486	    # single generator token. Reporting the classifier population restores FX-06
487	    # equality by construction while the on-topic gate honesty lives entirely in the
488	    # dedicated on_topic_* fields. The decision is byte-identical (it never reads these
489	    # two reported fields — they are artifact/approval-population fields only).
490	    reported_tier_counts = dict(tier_counts)
491	    reported_total_sources = sum(reported_tier_counts.values())
492	    return CorpusAdequacyReport(
493	        decision=decision,
494	        findings=findings,
495	        total_sources=reported_total_sources,
496	        # REPORTED population = the classifier histogram (the population the approval
497	        # gate `dist` and the report consume). The on-topic counts the DECISION used
498	        # are surfaced separately via on_topic_tier_counts / on_topic_evidence_rows.
499	        tier_counts=reported_tier_counts,
500	        evidence_rows=grounded_evidence_rows,
501	        notes=notes,
502	        thresholds=asdict(thr),
503	        on_topic_evidence_rows=on_topic_grounded,
504	        raw_grounded_evidence_rows=raw_grounded,
505	        on_topic_relevance_floor=(_floor if evidence_rows is not None else 0.0),
506	        on_topic_tier_counts=dict(on_topic_tier_counts),
507	    )
508

1 """ 2 Phase 2g — Corpus-approval gate. 3 4 After retrieval and tier classification, the pipeline stops and shows 5 the user the final corpus tier distribution alongside the expected 6 distribution from `protocol.json`. The user must EXPLICITLY approve 7 before synthesis begins. 8 9 ADDRESSES PG_LB_SA_02_CONTENT_AUDIT Section E-05: the pre-rebuild 10 pipeline went from retrieval directly to synthesis with no user- 11 visible checkpoint. Users saw the final report but could not see what 12 corpus produced it. Corpora dominated by T5 industry marketing + T6 13 blog posts were synthesized into confident-sounding claims. 14 15 RUBBER-STAMP RESISTANCE (FX-05 / I-ready-017): 16 - If actual distribution is >=15 percentage points off the expected 17 in any single tier, the gate blocks auto-approve. 18 - A material-deviation corpus is DENIED by default 19 (abort_corpus_approval_denied, no generator tokens billed). A free-text 20 note is NOT a sanctioned credential — the R-3 sweep's own 50-char canned 21 note defeated the old len>=30 + denylist heuristic and billed a 22 material-deviation corpus. 23 - The ONLY sanctioned auto-approve is a structured operator authorization 24 (env/CLI PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL=1), recorded as an audit-logged 25 AuthorizedSweep{authorized_by, authorized_at, flag_source}. 26 """ 27 28 from __future__ import annotations 29 30 import hashlib 31 import json 32 import logging 33 import os 34 import time 35 from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field 36 from pathlib import Path 37 from typing import Any, Optional 38 39 logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.corpus_approval_gate") 40 41 # Deviation threshold (absolute percentage points) — a tier whose 42 # actual fraction is more than this far from its expected bounds is 43 # flagged as "material deviation" requiring an explicit opt-in note. 44 PG_TIER_MATERIAL_DEVIATION_PP = float( 45 os.getenv("PG_TIER_MATERIAL_DEVIATION_PP", "15.0") 46 ) / 100.0 47 48 49 @dataclass 50 class CorpusSource: 51 """A single retrieved source after tier classification.""" 52 53 url: str 54 tier: str # "T1".."T7" or "UNKNOWN" 55 title: str = "" 56 domain: str = "" 57 tier_confidence: float = 0.0 58 tier_rule: str = "" 59 tier_reasons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) 60 # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311): per-citation CONTENT-RELEVANCE weight surfaced to the 61 # user (§-1.3 weight-not-filter). Default 1.0 = full weight / W2 OFF, so this 62 # is byte-identical for every existing caller (additive optional field). A 63 # demoted (off-topic) source carries a low weight here — it is NEVER dropped. 64 content_relevance_weight: float = 1.0 65 content_relevance_label: str = "" # "" when W2 OFF; e.g. "relevant"/"demoted" 66 67 68 # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311) P1-1: the two W2 fields above are ADDITIVE to CorpusSource, 69 # but a plain `asdict(...)`/json dump emits them EVEN AT DEFAULTS — breaking the 70 # "byte-identical when W2 OFF" kill-switch contract (wiring_standard pt 13) for 71 # every serialized corpus snapshot (live_corpus_dump.json, corpus_snapshot.json, 72 # fetch_snapshot.json, corpus_approval.json). FIX: a custom `dict_factory` that 73 # OMITS the two W2 keys whenever they hold their defaults (weight == 1.0 AND 74 # label == ""), so a W2-OFF source serializes BYTE-IDENTICAL to base, and a 75 # W2-ON demoted source still surfaces its weight + label. The omission is keyed on 76 # the VALUE being default (not on the flag) so it is correct even across a snapshot 77 # round-trip. Recursive nested dataclasses (CorpusApprovalDecision -> 78 # CorpusDistributionReport -> ... and the CorpusSource list) all flow through this 79 # factory when passed to `asdict(..., dict_factory=_corpus_dict_factory)`. 80 _W2_DEFAULT_WEIGHT = 1.0 81 _W2_DEFAULT_LABEL = "" 82 83 84 def _corpus_dict_factory(items: "list[tuple[str, Any]]") -> "dict[str, Any]": 85 """`asdict` dict_factory that drops the W2 content-relevance keys at default. 86 87 `asdict` calls this with the (key, value) pairs of EACH dataclass it visits 88 (recursively). For a CorpusSource at W2 defaults this omits the two W2 keys so 89 the dict is byte-identical to the pre-W2 schema; any other dataclass is 90 unaffected (it carries no such keys). A non-default (demoted) source keeps both 91 keys so the W2 effect remains visible in the serialized corpus. 92 """ 93 d = dict(items) 94 if ( 95 d.get("content_relevance_weight", _W2_DEFAULT_WEIGHT) == _W2_DEFAULT_WEIGHT 96 and d.get("content_relevance_label", _W2_DEFAULT_LABEL) == _W2_DEFAULT_LABEL 97 ): 98 d.pop("content_relevance_weight", None) 99 d.pop("content_relevance_label", None) 100 return d 101 102 103 def corpus_asdict(obj: Any) -> Any: 104 """`asdict(obj)` that omits the W2 content-relevance keys at their defaults. 105 106 Use this at EVERY CorpusSource / CorpusApprovalDecision serialization site so a 107 W2-OFF run produces byte-identical JSON to base (P1-1).""" 108 return asdict(obj, dict_factory=_corpus_dict_factory) 109 110 111 @dataclass 112 class TierDeviation: 113 """Describes how actual fraction compares to expected range.""" 114 115 tier: str 116 actual_fraction: float 117 expected_min: float 118 expected_max: float 119 deviation_pp: float # signed: positive = above max, negative = below min 120 is_material: bool # True if |deviation_pp| > threshold 121 122 123 @dataclass 124 class CorpusDistributionReport: 125 """Summary of actual tier mix vs protocol-expected mix.""" 126 127 total_sources: int 128 tier_counts: dict[str, int] 129 tier_fractions: dict[str, float] 130 deviations: list[TierDeviation] 131 has_material_deviation: bool 132 auto_approve_allowed: bool 133 134 135 @dataclass 136 class AuthorizedSweep: 137 """Structured operator authorization to auto-approve a material-deviation 138 corpus (FX-05 / I-ready-017). 139 140 This REPLACES the old free-text "why is this corpus acceptable" note as the 141 auto-approve credential. A free-text field is defeatable (the R-3 sweep's own 142 48-char canned note passed the len>=30 + denylist guard → a material-deviation 143 corpus auto-approved and was billed). A credential must be a *positive, 144 audit-logged acknowledgement keyed to an explicit flag*, not prose. 145 146 Built only by `authorization_from_env()` when `PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL` 147 is set truthy. Persisted as a structured block in `corpus_approval.json`. 148 """ 149 150 authorized_by: str # identity/source, e.g. "env:PG_AUTHORIZED_SWEEP_APPROVAL" 151 authorized_at: str # ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the authorization was minted 152 flag_source: str # provenance of the flag: "env" | "cli" 153 154 155 @dataclass 156 class CorpusApprovalDecision: 157 """Persisted once the user approves / rejects the corpus.""" 158 159 run_id: str 160 decision_at_unix: float 161 decision_at_iso: str 162 approved: bool 163 user_note: str = "" 164 approved_source_urls: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) 165 rejected_source_urls: list[str] = field(default_factory=list) 166 report: Optional[CorpusDistributionReport] = None 167 protocol_sha256: str = "" 168 # FX-05: the STRUCTURED credential that authorized an auto-approve on a 169 # material-deviation corpus (None = no structured authorization; for a 170 # material-deviation corpus that means the run was/should be denied). 171 authorization: Optional[AuthorizedSweep] = None 172 173 174 def compute_tier_distribution( 175 sources: list[CorpusSource], 176 protocol: dict[str, Any], 177 ) -> CorpusDistributionReport: 178 """Aggregate the corpus tier mix vs the pre-registered protocol. 179 180 Args: 181 sources: List of retrieved sources with Phase 2a tier 182 assignments already applied. 183 protocol: Dict form of protocol.json (must have key 184 'expected_tier_distribution' — list of dicts with 185 tier / min_fraction / max_fraction). 186 """ 187 total = len(sources) 188 counts: dict[str, int] = {} 189 for s in sources: 190 counts[s.tier] = counts.get(s.tier, 0) + 1 191 192 fractions = { 193 k: (v / total if total > 0 else 0.0) 194 for k, v in counts.items() 195 } 196 197 deviations: list[TierDeviation] = [] 198 expected_list = list(protocol.get("expected_tier_distribution") or []) 199 for entry in expected_list: 200 tier = entry.get("tier") 201 if not tier: 202 continue 203 exp_min = float(entry.get("min_fraction", 0.0)) 204 exp_max = float(entry.get("max_fraction", 1.0)) 205 actual = fractions.get(tier, 0.0) 206 207 if actual < exp_min: 208 dev_pp = actual - exp_min 209 elif actual > exp_max: 210 dev_pp = actual - exp_max 211 else: 212 dev_pp = 0.0 213 214 is_material = abs(dev_pp) > PG_TIER_MATERIAL_DEVIATION_PP 215 deviations.append(TierDeviation( 216 tier=tier, 217 actual_fraction=round(actual, 4), 218 expected_min=exp_min, 219 expected_max=exp_max, 220 deviation_pp=round(dev_pp, 4), 221 is_material=is_material, 222 )) 223 224 any_material = any(d.is_material for d in deviations) 225 226 return CorpusDistributionReport( 227 total_sources=total, 228 tier_counts=counts, 229 tier_fractions={k: round(v, 4) for k, v in fractions.items()}, 230 deviations=deviations, 231 has_material_deviation=any_material, 232 auto_approve_allowed=not any_material, 233 ) 234 235 236 def render_approval_html( 237 report: CorpusDistributionReport, 238 sources: list[CorpusSource], 239 research_question: str, 240 ) -> str: 241 """Render a self-contained HTML page for human corpus review. 242 243 Deliberately minimal (no JS) so it works in any text-only 244 browser.
